Election Procedure to Elect an ICANN Director
Date
14 September 2011
At its meeting on the 26 June 2003 the ICANN Board adopted the amendments to the bylaws under resolution 03.106 Bylaw Article IX - section 3.9 which states:
"The ccNSO Council shall make selections to fill Seats 11 and 12 on the Board by written ballot or by action at a meeting; any such selection must have affirmative votes of a majority of all the members of the ccNSO Council then in office. Notification of the ccNSO Council's selections shall be given by the ccNSO Council Chair in writing to the ICANN Secretary, consistent with Article VI, Sections 8(4) and 12(1)."
The ccNSO Council and ccNSO members have decided that before making the nomination of a Director, the ccNSO members shall be entitled to vote for the nominee they would like to see on the ICANN Board.
Summary
Only ccNSO members will be eligible to nominate or second a candidate for seat 12 on the ICANN Board.
Only ccNSO members will be eligible to vote in the election of a candidate.
Nominations may be made by a member and seconded by a member.
Voting will be by secret email ballot, that is, the ballot is identified only by a code and not by a name.
The email voting will be ratified by the Election Manager.
An electoral list will be published.
TIMELINE
15 September 2011: Nominations period starts.
6 October 2011: Nominations Process closes
13 October 2011: Acceptance of Nomination by Candidate. Announcement of nominees. If there are two or more candidates, an election will be held. Candidates are invited to submit mission statements.
25 October 2011: End of period for submitting mission statements; Public Q&A session at ccNSO members meeting
26 October 2011: If only one candidate: Approval of nominee by ccNSO Council; If elections needed:
10 November 2011: Call for votes, ballots issued
24 November 2011: Closure of voting
1 December 2011 (latest): Announcement of election results In any ties, run off elections announced (timeline to be defined)
Call for Nominations
Open: 15th September 2011 at 00:00 UTC Close: 6th October 2011 at 00:00 UTC
For other places see world clock
Nominations should be sent by email to the Election Manager at the following email address:
ccnso-boardnominations@icann.org, in the form described in the Call for Nominations.
Archives to be found at: http://forum.icann.org/lists/ccnso-boardnominations
Each member can nominate one candidate for seat 12 on the ICANN Board and each member can second one candidate.
In order to nominate or second a candidate, a member must be a member of the ccNSO by the 15th September 2011.
Nomination procedures with timeline, template, email for posting and URL of archives of nominations will be posted.
The Election Manager shall confirm the list of the Nominees before the voting period starts.
Procedure for Voting by the ccNSO Members
Each member will be entitled to vote for up to one candidate.
Section 2(3) of Article VI of the Bylaws states
"In carrying out their responsibilities to fill Seats 9 through 14, the Supporting Organizations shall seek to ensure that the ICANN Board is composed of members that in the aggregate display diversity in geography, culture, skills, experience, and perspective, by applying the criteria set forth in Section 3 of this Article. At any given time, no two Directors selected by a Supporting Organization shall be citizens of the same country or of countries located in the same Geographic Region."
The candidate that receives the highest number of votes from members will be nominated by the Council to the ICANN Board.
Voting Period and Method
The ccNSO members will vote by email
Voting will open: 10 November 2011, at 00:00 UTC Voting will close: 24 November 2011, at 00:00 UTC
For other places see world clock.
The full record of the number of votes will be published at the end of selection process.
Results
The election results will be announced by the Election Manager.

BitLife How to Escape Every Prison Guide – All Jail Layouts, Houdini & Jailbird Ribbon!
By: Andrew Vaughan, Vignesh Raja - Updated: April 9, 2025
59
Know how to escape every Prison Layout in BitLife!
Image via Candywriter
Our BitLife how to escape every Prison Guide features maps and videos for every prison layout you'll encounter! We've got cheat sheet maps for all the layouts in the game. We'll help you quickly complete ribbons, including Houdini and Jailbird, and everything else about escaping prison in BitLife. Recommended Videos    Table of Contents     How to Escape Every Prison in Bitlife (2024)    What is a Prison Layout in BitLife?    All Maximum Security Prison Layouts    Maximum Security Layout #1 - 8x8    Maximum Security Layout #2 - 8x8    Maximum Security Layout #3 - 8x8    Maximum Security Layout #4 - 8x8    Maximum Security Layout #5 - 8x8    Maximum Security Layout #6 - 8x8    Maximum Security Layout #7 - 8x8    Maximum Security Layout #8 - 8x8    Maximum Security Layout #9 - 8x7    Normal Security Prison Layouts    Normal Prison Layout #1 - 6x6    Normal Prison Layout #2 - 5x5    Normal Prison Layout #3 - 6x6    Normal Prison Layout #4 - 5x4    Normal Prison Layout #5 - 6x6    Normal Prison Layout #6 - 5x5    Normal Prison Layout #7 - 7x4    Normal Prison Layout #8 - 7x5    Normal Prison Layout #9 - 6x5    Normal Prison Layout #10 - 7x5    Normal Prison Layout #11 - 4x4    Normal Prison Layout #12 4x4    How to get all Bitlife Prison Ribbons    BitLife Jailbird Ribbon    BitLife Houdini Ribbon    More Posts from PGG    How to Escape Every Prison in Bitlife (2024) Escaping prison in BitLife is difficult, especially if you're just starting the game. The key to getting out is to counter the police guard's movement and not allow him to get near your tile.  The guard moves twice for every move you make! He will only make moves towards you and will try to walk horizontally first.  Here's a valuable tip: the police guard will always attempt to move horizontally first. This predictable behavior can be used to our advantage by strategically blocking the cop against walls in the prison. Even if the police officer is directly below you and you move to the right or left, he will always move horizontally first. That means if there's a wall above him afterward, he will not be able to reach you. If you want to complete these on your own, then this aspect is what you will be taking advantage of the most. If you need more help to escape every prison in BitLife, keep reading because we have a cheat sheet for each map layout and a video demonstrating how to complete them! What is a Prison Layout in BitLife? You will receive one of the following layouts when attempting to escape prison. These puzzles can be mirrored by being flipped around horizontally or vertically. It will be the same layout; you'll need to follow the steps based on the one you've received. If you fail, you can always move ahead a year and try again! Map Legend  Star: Your starting point. Arrow: Designates the direction you should be traveling at that point of the line.  All Maximum Security Prison Layouts Below are all the maximum prison security layouts in the game and the correct ways to find the exit door. Maximum Security Layout #1 - 8x8 This requires a lot of backtracking. You start from the left tile, go towards the top, and back again before moving to the other side and repeating a similar process before exiting. It is a challenge; refer to the image for the exact step-by-step move. Image by Pro Game Guides Video Answer    Maximum Security Layout #2 - 8x8 Like the last one, this also involves backtracking several times in the horizontal direction before making for an exit from the bottom left. Image via Pro Game Guides Video Answer    Maximum Security Layout #3 - 8x8 This 8x8 maze is very challenging to traverse; you must hide a lot at the top two L-shaped brackets before making an anti-clockwise move to exit from the right bottom.    Maximum Security Layout #4 - 8x8 In this 8x8, the exit is right in the center. Our aim should be to make the cop go as low as possible. Start by creating an anti-clock rotation to bait the cop in the downward direction. Once the guard is stuck in a wall, rotate clockwise to escape the prison.    Maximum Security Layout #5 - 8x8 A right top-hand side exit. Sneak around the bottom left walls to make the security go in that direction while you make an anti-clockwise rotation to find an escape from the jail.    Maximum Security Layout #6 - 8x8 A simple 8x8 layout that is easy to traverse due to your spawn is at the central part of the screen. Rotate along the left side of the layout and then immediately go downwards to find an exit.    Maximum Security Layout #7 - 8x8 To escape this layout, move slowly at the center, one tile at a tile, making an anti-clock rotation before exiting to the top left.    Maximum Security Layout #8 - 8x8 This is tricky; you have to move more slowly in the center, using the multiple walls' protection, before moving from the right bottom.    Maximum Security Layout #9 - 8x7 It is the most accessible maximum security layout. You start from the center and go clockwise, then move another circle anti-clockwise and exit from the left of the design.     Normal Security Prison Layouts Below are the regular layouts in the game. They are much easier to use than the maximum type layouts above. Normal Prison Layout #1 - 6x6 The first layout we're looking at requires looping back on your path, but it isn't too hard. Image via Pro Game Guides Normal Prison Layout #2 - 5x5 This one isn't too difficult. You need to do one quick double back and then a longer one as you get deeper into the map. Just don't make a mad dash for the Exit—you won't make it! Image by Pro Game Guides Normal Prison Layout #3 - 6x6 This is another pretty detailed one; you must do some backtracking basically around the left, upper and right-hand sides of the layout as shown in the diagram below. On your second time heading clockwise, you'll begin to cut in towards the middle of the map below the L-shaped walls in the upper middle section. Head around the right hand side of the lower L-shaped wall, left below it, and then down towards the exit, Image by Pro Game Guides Video Answer    Normal Prison Layout #4 - 5x4 This is probably the most uncomplicated layout from which to escape. It's very straightforward and only requires eight steps to complete. Image via Pro Game Guides Normal Prison Layout #5 - 6x6 This one is pretty long, and you'll be backtracking quite often. Make sure you do all the small backtracks listed on the map, or you won't get the cop into the right place. Image via Pro Game Guides Video Answer    Normal Prison Layout #6 - 5x5 This 5x5 layout makes for some pretty close-quarters encounters with the guard, and you'll need to do a fair amount of going back and forth to complete this one. Image via Pro Game Guides Video Answer    Normal Prison Layout #7 - 7x4 This is an easy one! All you need to do is get the police officer into the little U-shaped walls. Once you've done that, you can breeze through to the exit. Image via Pro Game Guides Normal Prison Layout #8 - 7x5 It's a pretty standard one. You'll have to trek across the jail several times to get the cop where you want him. Once you've done that, you can stroll your way out of prison! Image via Pro Game Guides Video Answer    Normal Prison Layout #9 - 6x5 You'll have to do a big loop and then return to keep the officer off you and get to the exit. Image via Pro Game Guides Normal Prison Layout #10 - 7x5 This is another one where you're doing quite a bit of backtracking, especially in the bottom right corner, where you'll need to go back and forth to get the cop in the right place. Be patient and deliberate, and you should be able to get to the exit! Image via Pro Game Guides Video Answer    Normal Prison Layout #11 - 4x4 The smallest current layout requires some back-and-forth to escape. This one is pretty easy! Image via Pro Game Guides Normal Prison Layout #12 4x4 This one is the easiest of all. Take a right and move from the top, evading the cop to find the exit on the left. Image via Pro Game Guides How to get all Bitlife Prison Ribbons Follow the instructions below to obtain the Jailbird and Houdini Ribbons. BitLife Jailbird Ribbon This one is easy. You must go in and out of prison about three or four times. Once you've done that, sit in your cell and age until you croak! That should be all you need to do to earn the Jailbird Ribbon! BitLife Houdini Ribbon You'll want to go to jail many times and escape immediately. You can level yourself up, not doing too much, and get to your 60s. Once you do that, start robbing houses and get caught. That will put you in jail, and you'll want to escape using the guide above. Do this a couple of times a year in your old age, and that should get you to the end and should get you the Houdini ribbon! For more on Bitlife, we at PGG have you covered with guides like How to join the mob, and How to become a mafia boss.  More Posts from PGG

in:
BitLife, Activities, Features, Gameplay
Activities
Sign in to edit
History
Purge
Talk (0)
Activities
Launched on iOS:
September 2018 (default)
Launched on Android:
February 2019 (default)
Activities are one of the most important mechanics of BitLife, as from here the player will have access to further gameplay, which gives your character things to do and shape them for Ribbons. Activities are greyed out if the character is too young to do them, and will stop being greyed out when they reach the minimum age to do these activities. Many activities can have mixed results and some include scenarios with them. Some actions are dependent on the nation your character is from, such as the driving age per nation, and the laws on doing gambling. You can add activities into Favourite Activities in the Settings which put them at the very top, which can reduce time scrolling and searching for an activity you commonly do.
Activity
Description
Options
Minimum age to do activities
Accessories
Accessorize yourself
Putting on eyewear or headwear prompts the achievement "Fancypants".
Eyewear (Put on some eyewear): You can put on eyewear that can be obtained in Challenges or Scavenger Hunts.
Headwear (Put on some headwear): You can put on headwear that can be obtained in Challenges or Scavenger Hunts.
0
Adoption
Adopt a child
Pick from up to 6 juveniles to adopt. Each have their own price, stats, looks, and reason why their birth parent(s) could not keep them. Some may be from foreign Nations.
18 (available)
21 (official)
Crime
Commit a crime
Bank Robbery
Burglary
Delinquent
Embezzle*
Grand theft auto
Hitman
Murder
Pickpocket
Porch pirate
Shoplift
Train Robbery
* You must have a full-time job in order to embezzle.
8 (Delinquent, Pickpocket, Porch Pirate, Shoplift)
10 (Burglary)
14 (Grand Theft Auto and Murder)
16 (Bank Robbery, Embezzle, and Train Robbery)
18 (Hitman)
Doctor
Visit the doctor
Alternative Doctor (Seek alternative medicine)
Doctor (Seek western medicine): You can choose between two different doctors, each with varying levels of quality. If you do not have a disease/ailment, the doctor will not treat you. If you do, the doctor will treat you, and there is a chance your disease/ailment gets cured. It is free to have an appointment with a doctor or be treated by them in certain countries.
Donate Blood (Make a blood donation):
Donate Plasma (Make a plasma donation):
Emergency Room (Visit the emergency room): You can go there and be treated. Being treated in the emergency room would increase your health if it is low. It is free to be treated in the emergency room in certain countries.
Optometrist (Visit the optometrist): The optometrist can only be accessed with Bitizenship. You can select to put on different spectacles or contact lenses there.
Psychiatrist (Visit a shrink):
Witch Doctor (Seek the last resort): You can get the Witchcraft achievement if your disease is cured after being prescribed by the witch doctor.
4 (Doctor and Emergency Room)
18 (Alternative Doctor, Psychiatrist, Witch Doctor, Blood, Plasma Donation)
Emigrate
Move to another country
The player will have an option of eight nations to emigrate to.
18 (19 in countries where the high school graduation age is 19 unless dropped out)
Fame (Only available if famous)
Manage your fame
Book
Commercial
Photo Shoot
Talk Show
18 (Though you'd usually need to be a little older to gain enough fame)
Fertility
Visit a fertility clinic
Birth Control (Female)
IVF
Sperm Donor(Females only)
Tubal Ligation (Female)
Vasectomy (Male)
Sperm Donation(Males only)
14 (Birth Control)
18 (IVF, Sperm Donor, Tubal Ligation/Vasectomy,Sperm Donation)
Gamble
Visit the casino
Choose one of two casinos (casino names are randomized) and play minigames of Blackjack. In some countries, gambling is illegal.
18
Horse Races
Go to the horse track
Choose to bet from five horses or just watch.
18
Identity
Define your identity
Gender (Identify your gender): When clicked, a tab will open stating your character's desired gender and their birth gender. The player can choose to identify as cisgender (same as birth gender), genderqueer (gender that constantly changes/existing outside of male/female), non-binary (neither male or female), or transgender male/or female (not the same as birth gender). Identifying as transgender prompts your character to change your name to one suiting your gender.
Name Change
Sexuality
4 (Gender)
Lawsuit
Sue someone
Your character can sue surgeons that botch your plastic surgery, people who assault you, or police who falsely accuse you of crime.
18
Licences
Manage your licenses
Get a driver's, pilot's, or boating licence. The game will show a traffic sign, plane signal, or boating scenario and the player will have to choose the correct meaning of it to pass the exam. Before getting a pilot's licence, your character must attend flight school and get at least 40 hours.
Dependent on the legal ages in the country your character is from.
(15-19)
18 (all other licenses)
Lottery
Play the lottery
Your character can buy lottery tickets to win a jackpot prize. They may buy 1 or 10 tickets. Winning the lottery is rare, but doing so wins the "Jackpot" achievement.
18
Love
Find someone to love
Date
Dating app
Hookup
Threesome
Mail order bride(Males only)
14 (Date)
18 (Dating app, Hookup, Threesome,Mail order bride)
Mind & Body
Work on self-improvement
Acting Lessons
Book (Find a good book): The player is given a selection of seven different books varying in length. After choosing a book, a tab states the title, the author, the genre, and the amount of pages it has. Tapping the book flips one page. After tapping it as much as the number of pages in the book, you can finish the book, which increases your smarts and happiness. You can also choose to abandon the book or pick a different book, as well as watching the movie the book is associated with, which requires you to watch an ad if you don't have Bitizenship. Finishing the dictionary prompts the achievement "Human Dictionary".
Diet
Gardening
Gym
Instruments: You can pay to take lessons to learn how to play certain instruments, or ask your parents to let your character take instrument lessons, which they can decline if they have little money or if you already play a lot of different instruments. After taking a lesson, your skill on the instrument would improve/worsen (which happens if you take too many lessons in the same year), and your parents can buy you instruments if you are under 18. When the skill bar is full, you "master" it. These are all the different instruments you can choose to take lessons on:
Accordion;
Banjo;
Bass Guitar;
Cello;
Didgeridoo (mastering it prompts the "Didgeridoo'er" achievement);
Drums;
Flute;
Guitar;
Harmonica;
Harp;
Kazoo;
Keyboard;
Organ;
Pan Flute (mastering it prompts the "Pan Flute Pro" achievement);
Piano;
Recorder;
Saxophone;
Steel Drum;
Tambourine;
Triangle;
Trombone;
Trumpet;
Tuba;
Violin.
Library
Martial Arts
Meditate
Memory Test (Have your memory evaluated): The player can play a minigame where they must repeat the sequence of flashing lights and colors. The sequence gets longer for each correct repeated sequence, making the game harder. Scoring less than 10 correct sequences will decrease smarts, while scoring more than 10 will increase it. Scoring a 20 on the memory test prompts the achievement "Hyperthymesia".
Modeling Lessons
Space Camp
Walks
Voice Lessons
6 (Book, Instruments, Memory Test)
8 (Walk, Martial Arts,Acting&Voice Lessons)
12 (Garden, Gym, Library, Meditation)
18 (Diet)
Movie Theater
Go to the movies
Watching a movie prompts the achievement "Moviegoer". Watching five movies prompts the achievement "Movie Junkie". Watching over twenty movies throughout your life gets the player the "Movie Buff" ribbon.
Comedy (Go see a comedy):
Documentary (Go see a documentary):
Family (Go see a children's film): You can watch a film with your family, which increases happiness.
6-17 (Family)
18 (Comedy, Documentary)
Nightlife
Go clubbing
Choose from 3 nightclubs.
18
Pets
Get a pet
Animal Shelter
Cat Breeders
Dog Breeders
Horse Ranch (The player must own property with the ranch icon first; like an acreage, equestrian property, or ranch)
Llama Farm (The player must own property with the ranch icon first; like an acreage, equestrian property, or ranch)
Exotic Pet Dealer
Pet Store
6 (Animal Shelter, Cat Breeders, Dog Breeders)
15 (On your own)
18 (Exotic Pets, Horse Ranch/Llama Farm)
Plastic Surgery
Get plastic surgery
Botox
Brazilian Butt Lift (Female)
Breast Enlargement (Female)
Eyelid Surgery
Face Lift
Liposuction
Penis Enlargement Surgery (Male)
Sex Change Surgery
Tummy Tuck
18
Pray
Pray to the BitLife devs
To pray, you need to choose a subject to pray about and watch an ad. Alternatively, you can choose to 'curse them instead,' which usually ends up breaking the game. Currently, you can pray about six subjects: Fertility, General, Happiness, Health, Love, and Wealth. You need to be at least 18 years old to pray. When it was first introduced, you could pray for a clue about the next update. However, when BitLife announced the update, this option was removed.
Rack Tracks
Go to a race track
Rehab
Battle your addictions
Alcoholics Anonymous
Hypnotherapy
Rehab Center
18
Royalty (Only available if in the royal family)
Manage your royal life
Abdicate
Celebrity
Execute (Only available when a monarch)
Honorific
Law Review (Only available when in a monarchical family)
Public Disservice
Public Service
6 (Honorific, Law Review, Public Disservice, Public Service)
18 (Abdicate, Celebrity, Execute)
Salon & Spa
Take time for yourself
Barber
Dye Job
Massage
Nail Salon
Tanning Salon
Waxing Salon
Shave (Male only)
14 (Barber, Nail Salon)
18 (Dye Job, Massage, Tanning Salon, Waxing Salon)
21 (Shave)
Shopping
Go shopping
Redirects to "Shopping" page under Assets.
18
Social Media
Manage your online identity
Surrender...
N/A
To surrender, the player will end their character's life, ending their journal with "I surrendered" and giving the Wasteful ribbon. Before choosing to surrender, the game will warn "Don't end the life of (character name)!" and provide the option to continue their life or to surrender. Previously known as "End Life," the journal ended with "I ended my life," and the character would not receive the Wasteful ribbon unless they surrendered at age 0 or contributed little to their life.
0
Sweepstakes
Enter the sweepstakes
In order to enter the sweepstakes, you must pay an in-game price starting from $1. When you win, you will receive a push notification saying you have won the sweepstakes. You can win a car, boat, jewelry, or other in-game assets with sweepstakes. You need to be at least 18 years old in order to enter the sweepstakes.
Time Machine
Travel back in time
You can choose how many years back you would like to travel, starting from 1 year, 3 years, 5 years, 10 years, 15 years, or 20 years. A payment of $1 is required each time.
1
Vacation
Go on a vacation
Cruise
Vacation
18
Will/Testament
Manage your legacy
You can decide to whom to leave what your character has (money, houses, heirlooms, etc.). You can choose to divide it equally, leave it all to one specific person, or donate it to charity. Your relatives may argue with you if you leave them out of your will.
18
Zoo Trip
Go to the zoo

BitLife Review
Posted on 11/01/202110/29/2021 by Core Reviews
Released: September 29th, 2018
Developer: Candywriter
Genre: Simulator
Platform: Mobile (iOS, Android)
I know you guys are like FINALLY A GAME REVIEW (but not Bustafellows, I’m sorry) BUT I do have another to talk with you guys about. Besides Otome games, I LOVE playing simulation games. My favorite is probably Sims. I love any game where I can make choices that affect how the game goes, you know what I mean? I distinctly remember playing a game similar to this except it was only available on a web browser. I can’t recall the name but it was basically a text simulation game like this one is. You were born, you had different life experiences, etc. EDIT: I just remembered it was called Alter Ego!
The game I’m focusing on today is called BitLife. It is a text simulator game where you basically make all the decisions and based on what you pick you to get different results. You start as a baby and you can age year by year learning new things, changing the way you look, it’s like your life. You can play it any way you like. I like to try to lead a semi-normal life but there’s really no limit to what you can do. You can even choose to be the head of a mafia family or lead a crime organization. There are good choices, which can give you great results and make your character happy, or there are bad choices that can leave you hurt, upset, or even worse, dead.
It’s basically like the sims except it’s all text. There are images and cute little icons but you can do the little mindless things you can in the sims like cooking or flooding your house because you can’t unclog your toilet.
My character Sasha Li
So, this is the character that I’ve created. This is actually my second run-through of the game. I played as her mother then switched over to play as her daughter, Sasha, and continued on from there. This is what the screen will look like. It displays your characters’ pictures, their name, their occupation, and their bank account balance. Underneath, it displays the text for all of the actions I’ve done. Then, below that is a little menu where you can see your job-related options, assets, what you own, your relationships, and activities you can do.
In the middle is a big ‘Age’ button where you can age your character up 1 year or in contrast, you can click the small minus button and age your character down a year (you can time travel if you pay, yaaaay). Underneath that is your stats bar. It displays your current happiness, health, smarts, and looks. It’s similar to the sims in that if your happy bar is too low then you won’t be able to do a lot. Same with your health, you will start getting sick and not be able to do certain activities.
As you can see, my character’s happiness is low because her mother passed away. I’ve also played where her ex-husband died, one of her children contracted a form of cancer and died when they were very young, and her pets also have passed away (that happens very often).
This is a more detailed profile of my character. I had played the game for about 2 days and had gotten this far. Funny story, my character actually started out as a professional volleyball player but after some controversy, she was let go and I decided to have her study to become a nurse.
When I tell yall this game can become so addicting and fun to read. I have played very mildly but I’m planning on making some outrageous choices next time just for the heck of it. As you can see, my character’s stats bar is pretty nice. Aside from her happiness, this is someone whose ex-husband has died, had a child die, recently lost both pets and her father and mother, she’s pretty emotionally worn down but she’s hanging in there.
The beauty of this game is that you can create whatever narrative you want and you can change that narrative whenever you want.
On my right, you can see where they display my family legacy. I am currently the second generation and we’re pretty wealthy too.
You can also inherit heirlooms and accumulate assets where you can pass them on to your children or even sell them just because.
Like I said, you really have the freedom to create whatever story or family you want. If you’re not all that concerned with outstanding graphics or 900 fps but I find it to be a good way to kill time if you’re into these types of games.
Gaming Checklist
Is it fun?
Yes. I know some of you may be like “hOw CaN a TeXt GaMe Be FuN?” Well it can! Trust me, if you can play games like Life or Cards Against Humanity, you can have fun with this too. You just have to tap into your
Is it expensive?
Nope! Actually it IS technically free to play unless you want to get some of the extra features (like time travel, creating your own characters, towns, cities, etc) then it’s only $4.99.
Is it difficult or easy?
It’s actually rather simple unless you think of it like the Sims then it can be a little challenging. Your goal is to essentially create an ideal life for your character rather that be just to get rich, live long, or find true love. There are certain choices that will lead you to that and it’s not always easy.
Is it family-friendly?
Ehhh, I think it’s just like real life; there’s just some topics it’s hard to shy away from so I wouldn’t really suggest younger kids to play it but maybe late teens.
With all that being said, Core Reviews is going to go ahead and give this text based game a rating of:
🍙🍙🍙
While it’s not a horrible game, it’s not the greatest either BUT I still enjoy it and think some people out there might as well. My recommendation? Give a play through and see how you feel about it. I’d suggest giving it a few tries before settling your opinion on it.
Thanks again for reading and as always please remember to like, leave a comment and share!
Share this:TweetShare on TumblrLike this:Like Loading...

There Is No Game is a computer game that claims to not be a game, but that's not actually true! Not only can you play it, but you can also beat it – you just have to know where to click on the screen and what objects to move around. This easy, step-by-step walkthrough will give you all the guidance you need to make it through every phase of There Is No Game, from the title screen to the Pong game to the goat rescue challenge!
How to Play There Is No Game
To beat There Is No Game, start by solving the puzzle on the title screen by spelling "GAME" on the letter board. Beat the Pong game by breaking the blocks to collect the trophy. Then, find a goat, grow a tree, and get the key from the squirrel to release the goat from its cage.
Steps
Section 1 of 4:
There Is No Game Walkthrough
Download Article
1
Solve the puzzle on the There Is No Game title screen. What makes this game so unique (and so confusing!) is the first puzzle in the game is actually the title screen itself. Players can figure out how to solve it by themselves simply by moving their mouse around and clicking on letters and buttons until something happens. The narrator gives you clues on what you should do next to progress. But if you want to cut to the chase and solve the title screen quickly, follow these steps.[1]
X
Research source
On the title screen, click the "O" in "NO" 10 times to make it fall. Then click and drag the "O" back up to the title to try to put it back in place. The letter will fall back down every time you let go of it and "shake" the screen. Repeat this step several times until a large volume button falls from the top right corner of the screen.[2]
X
Research source
Click on the volume button to mute the narrator. Next, the volume button will start jumping quickly to different parts of the screen. Follow it with your mouse and click it again. Then the narrator will trap the button in a wooden box, which you can break by dragging the "O" over it and dropping it. The narrator will then try trapping the volume button in a metal box. Pick up and drop the metal box with your mouse to "shake" the screen and cause all the letters in the title to fall down.[3]
X
Research source
With the letters on the ground, all that will be left in the middle of the screen is 2 screws. Unscrew them by left-clicking on them with your mouse. A panel will open on the screen and reveal a letter board that reads "THERE IS A” with 4 blank spaces after it. Drag and drop the letters that spell "GAME" up to the blank spaces on the letter board.[4]
X
Research source
2
Beat the Pong game and collect the trophy. After you beat the first title screen, the screen changes to a game of Pong with several rows of blocks. Underneath the blocks, you'll notice a horizontal bar with a ball stuck to it. Click and drag it around with your mouse to release the ball and bounce it up into the blocks. Each time the ball touches a block, it breaks it. Your goal is to break each block without letting the ball fall past the bar. Of course, since this is There Is No Game, there's a clever twist! Here's how to overcome it.
Drag the bar around the screen to destroy as many blocks as you can with the ball. After a while, the narrator will try to make it more difficult by moving the remaining blocks around. As you continue to break more blocks, the narrator will get frustrated and delete your ball.[5]
X
Research source
Replace the ball by dragging the ball from underneath your score on the left side of the screen. Let go of it so it bounces off the horizontal bar or the bottom of the screen, then destroy more blocks. Flashing colored blocks will appear on either side of the Pong screen as the game "glitches," but don't let that distract from your objective: eliminate all the blocks in the middle.[6]
X
Research source
When all the blocks are destroyed, a gleaming trophy will appear in the middle of the screen. Click on it to make it drop, then pick it up and drag it over to the blinking green arrow on the right side of the screen. You'll be taken back to the title screen, which is where you'll start the next puzzle.[7]
X
Research source
Advertisement
3
Find the goat and grow a tree to help you chase the flying squirrel. During the last stage of the Pong game, the narrator brainstorms things he thinks will make the game (which he keeps insisting isn't a real game at all) better. The last item he mentions is a goat, and this is an extremely important clue to keep in mind as you continue to progress. Here's how to find the goat and use it to advance.
When you return to the title screen after beating the Pong game, drop the trophy. This will cause the screen to "shake" and the letters on the letter board to fall on the ground. Pick up the letters that spell "GOAT" and arrange them on the letter board, then click the green arrow that appears on the right side of the screen.[8]
X
Research source
The arrow will take you to a new screen where you'll see a goat locked in a cage that's dangling from the ceiling. You'll also notice a waterfall to the goat's right. Nothing will happen if you click around, so your only alternative is to click the green arrow on the left to return to the title screen.[9]
X
Research source
After returning to the title screen, a flying squirrel with a key will appear and float to the top of the screen. The key is for the goat's cage, so you'll need to get it! To do that, replace the letters that spell "GOAT" on the letter board with the letters that spell "TREE." A tree will appear on the ground.[10]
X
Research source
Pick up the trophy and hover on the right-hand green arrow to go back to the cave where the goat is still trapped in its cage. Hold the trophy underneath the waterfall to fill it up with water. Then drag it back to the title screen and drop it above the tree. Repeat this process 3 times to make the tree grow tall enough that you can use it to follow the squirrel.[11]
X
Research source
4
Get the key and free the goat from its cage. When the top of the tree grows to reach the top of the screen, a new green arrow will appear next to it pointing upward. Click on it to change the screen to a sunny day with a blue sky, the top of the tree, and the pesky squirrel with the cage key perched on its leaves. Here's how you can persuade it to give up the key in exchange for a nut.
Find a brown nut on the right side of the tree top and click it until it falls down to the title screen. Follow it down, then click on the metal box from the first puzzle, drag it over the top of the nut, and drop it. The box will crack the nut's shell.[12]
X
Research source
Pick up and drag the shelled nut up to the screen with the squirrel and give it to the squirrel by holding it over his face. The squirrel will take the nut and drop the key down on to the title screen.[13]
X
Research source
Pick up and drag the key to the right to go back to the screen with the goat. Hold the key over the goat's cage. The cage will unlock and the goat will drop to the ground – but then, the screen will "glitch," and the narrator will scream, "No… the goat was a lie! A lie!!" Eventually, the screen will turn white, then fade to black.[14]
X
Research source
5
End the game by choosing if you want to forgive the narrator. After the goat screen fades to black, the narrator returns! He happily exclaims, "I'm not dead! I'm alive!" and then immediately expresses how he regrets glitching the game and putting you (the player) in danger. He asks you if you'll forgive him. Saying either "Yes" or "No" will end the game, but the narrator's dialogue changes depending on your response.[15]
X
Research source
If you say "No": The narrator will reply, "Go to hell!" Then the screen will, "Not a Game by Kamizoto."
If you say "Yes": The narrator will say, "Ho?! Really!? What a great news! Maybe we can be friends! No, no, that's not a good idea. We would get bored quickly together. Why? Because… as I said… THERE IS NO GAME!!!" Then the text changes to read, "Not a Game by Kamizoto."
Advertisement
Section 2 of 4:
How long does it take to beat There Is No Game?
Download Article
Players can beat the game in less than 10 minutes. If you know where and what to click on, you can finish the game in almost no time flat! If you decide not to use our walkthrough as a reference, though, it could take you 2-4 hours to figure out what to do to solve each puzzle! Both ways of playing the game are valid so it's up to you to decide which one sounds the most fun to you.
Section 3 of 4:
Where to Play There Is No Game
Download Article
1
Play There Is No Game for free online. You don't have to pay a cent for it, nor do you have to download any programs. The entire game is playable right from your web browser! Find it on Poki, CoolMathGames, or CrazyGames.
2
Buy the There Is No Game sequel on PC, Switch, or Android. If you beat There Is No Game but feel hungry for more, try out the sequel, There Is No Game: Wrong Dimension. Unlike the first installment, however, you do have to pay for WD. It costs $12.99 and is available for purchase on Steam and Nintendo Switch. You can also buy the app version from the Google Play Store for $4.99.
Advertisement
Section 4 of 4:
Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)
Download Article
1
What do you do with the goat in There Is No Game? The only thing you can do with the goat in There Is No Game is free it with the key you get from the flying squirrel. After you unlock the goat's cage, it drops to the ground, but freeing it is the only interaction you have with it.
2
How do you beat the squirrel in There Is No Game? To beat the squirrel, click on the nut on the right side of the tree where the squirrel is sitting several times until it falls. Follow it down to the title screen and break open the nut's shell by dropping the metal box on it. Pick up the shelled nut and carry it back up to the squirrel. The squirrel will take the nut and drop the key.
3
How do you crack the nut in There Is No Game? Crack the nut in There Is No Game by picking up the metal box from the first puzzle with your mouse and dropping it on the nut.
4
What's the end to There Is No Game? At the end of There Is No Game, the narrator glitches the game to try to end it. When the screen goes black, the narrator is surprised to find he's still alive. Then he apologizes to the player for putting them in danger and asks for their forgiveness. The player can either say "Yes" or "No." If they say "Yes," the narrator speaks a few more lines of dialogue and then the game ends. If they "No," the narrator says, "Go to hell!" and the game ends.

Is there more than one ending?
WikiWombatWatcher392
Community Answer
No, there are two endings. You can choose to forgive or not, and forgiving results in the narrator saying more lines of dialogue.

Genocide Guide
By Adam_Kocur
Welcome to Undertale, the lovable RPG where no one has to get hurt! For this guide, I will be giving you a step-by-step walkthrough explaining parts of the game and how to overcome them. Undertale, unlike many other games, has many unique endings and outcomes, depending on how you play the game and who you kill, so I will be going over the most merciless and brutal route of all, the Genocide ending. To achieve this ending, you must murder everyone in the game, which that includes more than just the enemies you encounter. To successfully accomplish this, you must kill the set amount of monsters for each which doing so requires you to walk around and randomly spawn enemy encounters, so you can't just truck thorugh the playthrough, you have to do lots of grinding. I'd also like to warn you that this route is filled with many heartbreaking deaths and feelings, so only persue this ending if you think you have the guts to. So without any further ado, let's get gaming!

The Ruins
Kills count: 20The game will start in the exact same way as in the Pacifist route. If you need any assistance with puzzles or gameplay, go to the Ruins section of my True Pacifist guide, and if you need help with fighting monsters, you can also use my Monsters guide for advise. Make sure you kill every monster in The Ruins before you engage Toriel, including all of those listed in the monsters guideYou must kill monsters until you get the "But nobody came" message. There are no significant differences between the Pacifist Ruins and Genocide ones, so just proceed as normal. You do not have to kill the Dummy or the first Froggit you meet with Toriel. Napstablook doesn't have to be killed, as he will disappear if you reach your kill limit before getting to him. You will notice that some NPCs are missing, such as some of the Froggits, because, of course, you killed them or they ran away.Where are the knives.You need 20 kills to clear the Ruins. Obtain all of them BEFORE you fight Toriel. You might have to walk around a lot to make encounters occur once you kill a good number of monsters. To try and make encounters happen, walk a little ways, then click out of the game so Frisk runs in place for you.Toriel killed. If you examine the cabinets in Toriel's kitchen, you will see "Where are the knives." When you're ready, fight Toriel. You'll kill her in one hit. If you go back into Toriel's home after you kill her, the fire will be gone, the flowers wilted, and, if you check her oven, it says "No one will use this anymore..." If you sleep in your bed, you will get a message as if you've got a Game Over, and then the music will be incredibly creepy in her house. If you look in the mirror, it says "It's me, [your name]."When you leave the Ruins, Flowey will say that you're not actually human. He has a plan to destroy all of the monsters, destroy all the memories once and for all. Head on into Snowdin for more victims.
Snowdin
Kill count: 16When you meet Sans in Snowdin, he notices that you don't bother to laugh at his prank on you. You will not go behind the lamp when Sans tells you to. When you try to leave the area, Sans comments that you're not human.Sans says you're just pretending to be human.In Snowdin, you will start to have a kill count that you can see in your Stat menu. Save points will tell you how many kills you need to purge an area of monsters. You need 16 to clear Snowdin. Do not engage Papyrus in the boss battle before you see the "But nobody came" message. You do NOT need to kill Jerry. Just flee from him or ditch him since it's not worth the effort.Dialogue will be different than the Pacifist route when you encounter Papyrus and Sans throughout this area. At the electric maze puzzle, you will walk right over the puzzle before Papyrus gets a chance to explain it. You won't have to complete some of the puzzles, such as the X and O puzzles. Papyrus will not bother to do the colored tile puzzle for you. When you talk to Sans after the long bridge, he warns you that you're going to have a bad time.You will simply take snow from the snowman instead of him giving you the piece. Feel free to just use the Snowman Piece as an item, since it has no use in this ending. You can keep taking pieces from him until he becomes just a pile of snow.Again, NPCs will be missing. There is no Nice Cream guy. Murderers don't get ice cream.Make sure to kill all the dog monsters you run into, including Doggo, Dogi, and Greater Dog. Note that if you kill Dogamy first, the other becomes enraged and their attacks become quite fast. If you kill Dogaressa first, Dogamy only attacks with one dog that barks in despair. You'll also encounter a new enemy called Chilldrake after you kill Snowdrake. To find enemies, I suggest checking around the area where you fight Dogi. Jerry battles will pop up here a lot with other monsters, but you can just ditch Jerry. Gryftots also give good EXP, usually found near the mystery door.If you visit Snowdin before you kill all the monsters, people will still be there and you can sleep in the inn. After you kill your quota, the town is empty except for Monster Kid. I suggest killing everyone first before buying something, as you can just steal the items from the shop after everyone evacuates. You can also steal over 700g from the shop! The save text will change to "that comedian..." if you arrive before killing everyone.When ready, head beyond Snowdin to face Papyrus. Again, you will move on your own, as if you're no longer in control of your character. Papyrus will try to convince you against your killing ways, and he will spare you immediately at the start of the fight. If you spare him, you abort the Genocide run. Again, you will kill him in one hit. Your intent to kill is too strong for these monsters. Papyrus insists he believes in you as he parishes. Proceed into Waterfall.
Waterfall
Kill count: 18Only Monster Kid will be at Sans's sentry. He's out to see Undyne, as usual. Once again, you need to kill all the monsters in Waterfall. The quota is 18 this time.There are no rocks coming down from the waterfall this time. Once again, Undyne will notice you when you walk into the grass, but she won't attack. Monster Kid will follow you, as well, as the area is eerily silent. The petal bridges are already formed for you as the monsters probably used them to escape, but you can still get the quiche if you want. All of the Echo Flowers are silent.Monster Kid complains that you climb roughly.Undyne will attack you by throwing spears, forcing you to dodge. See the Waterfall Pacifist section for more information. Again, Monster Kid saves you and runs ahead. You cannot use Sans's telescope, and there is no Nice Cream guy. You can still get the Dog Residue like earlier by doing the piano puzzle.Monster Kid will join you to walk along the puddles once again. When he let's you climb on his back, you'll do it roughly, making him complain a bit. You'll have to avoid Undyne's spears when she attacks, and then she will once more send you tumbling down into the dump. This time, you don't have to fight the Mad Dummy because apparently it gets so mad it instead becomes happy. Kill it anyway.Napstablook is not home and there are no snails to play with. Gerson is in his shop, though. Buy a Torn Notebook if you prefer its invincibility bonus. Talk to him for some cool information. Since he's a shopkeeper, you can't kill him. On the other hand, Temmie Village is abandoned. You can still sell your stuff here, but you cannot get Temmie Armor in a Genocide run. There is no offer to pay for Temmie's college tuition.Find a good place to grind near a save point so you can fulfill your kill quota. Monster KidUndyne will meet you again near the Echo Flower, and again Monster Kid interrupts. Continue to where Monster Kid meets you on the bridge. This time, your character will attack monster kid. When you check him, it says "Looks like free EXP."When you try to attack him, Undyne blocks the blow and is hit hard. Monster Kid flees, while Undyne looks like she's going to die. But Undyne never gives up! Her body keeps itself barely together, quivering with determination. This battle is one of the biggest, toughest in the game. I hope you're proud of what you've done.Boss: Undyne the UndyingCheck: 99ATK 99DEF Heroine reformed by her own DETERMINATION to save Earth.This battle works the same as the Pacifist fight with Undyne, except it's at least ten times harder. There is no holding back here. She will attack you hard and fast right away. Her attacks do insane damage and kill you in a hurry. You essentially need to avoid getting hit completely or you'll lose double digit HP per blow.Since this is Genocide, just attack her. She has a ton of HP! Unlike your opponents, she will not die easily, forcing you to block her attacks.Her arrows will vary in speed a lot more than the original fight. You need to anticipate which direction you should move first, and then think ahead. She will launch them at you insanely fast, but don't get intimidated. Just watch the pattern and judge her pace. She will use the yellow arrows as well that twist around to attack from the opposite side.When she turns you red, she will attack by throwing spears at you that are actually not as hard to avoid as blocking her arrows. Take advantage of this time to go all out. She will also spin spears around you. Just move through the gaps quickly and stay ahead of the pace. She will start attacking very fast with the spears, forcing you to move constantly. Try not to get overwhelmed. You need to keep moving, watching where the spears form and getting out of the way. Stay in the middle of the box to avoid getting cornered.There isn't much advice to give here except to learn her pattern as you die multiple times. She eventually will stop turning you green, meaning you've endured all of her arrow attacks, and then is your chance to hammer away.Once you defeat her, Undyne will melt away. She says that Alphys has already evacuated the monsters and Asgore has absorbed the souls. They're prepared for you. It's time now to advance to Hotland and see who else you can destroy.
Hotland
Kill count: 40Both Hotland and The Core are considered one area. This time, your quota is 40, a pretty large number, but you have more places to scour for kills.The Welcome sign is not working when you enter the area. Notice that there are no Royal Guard but you can't use the elevator yet. Walk through Hotland for items and kills. Inside Alphys's lab, you'll find Mettaton who informs you that Alphys has evacuated the monsters to a place you can't reach. Grab some noodles from her fridge and continue onward. Make sure to get the Burnt Pan here in Hotland as a useful item for your slaughter.All the lasers are already deactivated, and the large doors are already open. Again, you do not need to do any puzzles. Your intent to kill has forced the monsters to open everything and flee. Note that you cannot walk far from elevator's R2 and L2, as the area is blocked off.You'll encounter the Royal Guards just through the first big door who you of course must kill. You will also fight Muffet, but you'll kill her in one hit. In terms of grinding, try areas near the conveyor belts. If you walk against a belt going the other way, then click out of the game, you can walk in place and force encounters.At MTT Resort, take some items from Catty and Bratty's shop, like the gun and cowboy hat. Burgerpants is still there, and he gives you some use for the tons of gold you'll have.
The Core
In the Core, the elevator works, so you can skip the whole thing, but do not fight Mettaton before you get 40 kills. You can kill enemies along the bridge where the switch to open The End is.Mettaton waits for you inside the usual battle room. He will try to stop you from destroying humanity, then take on his true form as a human destroying robot.Check: 90 ATK 90 DEF Dr. Alphys's greatest invention.Once again, you will kill Mettaton in one hit. He will explode and remove the final barrier between you and the capital.
New Home
New Home is as quiet as always. There is no sign of anyone here, though the elevator is in use somehow. You once again need to get the keys in Asgore's home and head toward the throne room.You'll notice that a lot of the stuff you examine in Asgore's house reads in red text. You'll meet Flowey here, who will tell you about his story. He ended up in Asgore's garden, but realized he could not feel emotion and decided to take advantage of saving and resetting. Get the dagger and heart locket in your room and equip them. They have maximum stats, but the stats don't matter much at this point.When you reach the Last Corridor, save and take a deep breath. This is your final challenge. As you cross the corridor, Sans will stop you and ask you if a bad person can ever change. As you step forward, your bad time will begin.Final Boss: SansDon't let your guard down. Sans will start off with a little monologue, and then he will immediately dunk you as a blue soul onto the bottom of the box. Quickly jump back up! He then will immediately launch a string of bones at you, followed by multiple blasts from his dragon skeletons sometimes called blasters. Swerve gently through the bones, then move up, down to the center, and up again.You'll probably die in the first few seconds if you're not expecting it. You have to move as soon as the battle starts. Move out of the way of the direction the dragon's are facing. When two of them face opposite sides of the box, left and right, move all the way to the top. He will attack earlier in his dialogue if you die and come back a few times, so be on guard.Check: 1 ATK 1 DEF The easiest enemy. Can only deal 1 damage.He will let you have a turn after you make it past the first batch of attacks. You will notice that you have a purple bar near your HP called your Karmic Resistance. When Sans hits you, his attacks do residual damage that wears you down. Try to move quickly between turns so you have more HP to endure Sans's attacks. The purple bar drains up to where the yellow bar begins. Wait for the bar to stop draining before using healing items for maximum efficiency.This battle uses blue and white attacks like Papyrus. You must hold still for blue bones and move for white ones. The margin for error here is incredibly small. You have to find the right rhythm and recognize when he's making you jump low or high. Your jumps should just skim the top of the bones. Anything less accurate will likely hurt you.When you see a set of short bones on the bottom and long ones above, you need to jump into a gap between the small bones, move to the right side of the screen with them, then leap across once the top bones pass or jump between them.Platforms will line the top of bones sometimes. You need to jump on these and move from one to the other. Watch for bones coming in from the top! If you fall in the bones once, you'll probably die. Watch for where you need to jump and time your movements. Sometimes jumping isn't always the best option.He will start shooting at you with the skulls, forcing you to jump between platforms to dodge them. I suggest just not moving unless he threatens you with a skull and staying on the bottom, since moving down takes time. Jump up when a skull is about to shoot you, then drop back down. If two skulls threaten the same level, land on a platform above and then move back down quickly.This battle is really hard! Expect to die a lot. The key is to just stay calm. Get yourself out of danger and don't let the situation spiral out of control. It's ok if you get hit once. Think like Sans and stay chill.You just need to keep attacking him. Note that using an item or anything but attacking will not progress the battle. He will keep dodging your attacks, but just keep trying.He eventually will get worn out, then offer to spare you. Do not accept this offer! He will kill you immediately! This is the signal of a new phase in the fight. Take advantage of this respite to use items.He will start teleporting you to different scenarios, meaning you need to pay close attention to your movements. You just need to avoid damage until he flashes you to the next attack. A bone will hover over the side of your menu, meaning that you will take damage if you touch it. You need to time the way you use the menus. The bones will move down to the bottom bar, so now you have to look for openings to just navigate the bar. In fact, you're better off just not moving to the item menu at all if you can avoid it.This is when he starts dunking you by shoving you against the walls. You need to jump immediately after he slams you down to avoid his bones. When he moves bones up and down the side of the box, I suggest moving to the bottom of the box and just sliding back and forth.Eventually, Sans starts threatening to use his special attack, and he will launch one final, very long attack. He will turn you sideways and throw you into a gauntlet of bones. You have to quickly but smoothly swerve up and down until you slam against the end. Immediately jump to avoid his bones. Move out of the red boxes that flash around you. Then, a circle of dragon skulls will appear around you and shoot at you starting from the right side. Dip downward first, then hover closer to the center, but not too close, and move in a circle to avoid the laser blasts.After that, he will use his special attack, which is, literally, nothing. He just stands there and doesn't let you get a turn so you can't attack him. Just wait him out, even if it takes a few minutes, until he falls asleep. Then, move your box by forcing your soul against it to the Fight button and strike!Finally, you'll kill Sans, and he will walk off screen to apparently die. Or does he? It's not clear.With your last bit of opposition gone, continue on toward the throne room. Head to the coffins first for a rather creepy message, then head into the throne room. You'll meet Asgore there, apparently talking to Flowey. You'll enter an encounter with Asgore and kill him immediately without you having any control over it. Flowey will destroy his soul for you, and then plea for mercy from you. Again, you have no control over this. The spirit that has possessed you is in control now.Once you brutally kill Flowey, a new figure will meet you in a dark backdrop. Their name is the same as yours, though their official name is Chara. She thanks you for helping her come back to life. Your massacres helped fuel her reincarnation. She will suggest that you two should destroy the world together.IF YOU ANSWER HER QUESTION, YOUR GAME DATA WILL BE PERMANENTLY AFFECTED UNLESS YOU ARE WILLING TO COMPLETELY DELETE UNDERTALE FROM YOUR COMPUTER OR GO THROUGH THE PROCESS OF ERASING STEAM DATA. IF YOU DO NOT WANT TO DO THIS, CLOSE THE GAME AND RESET YOUR FILE.If you agree, she will happily destroy the world for you with one striking blow. Now you two will be partners forever! If you do not, she will jump scare you and destroy the world anyway. There isn't any way to avoid it at this point. Either way, your game will crash.When you relaunch the game, you will see nothing but blackness. You have to wait 10 minutes for Chara to show up again. Eventually, she will point out that you destroyed the world but, for some reason, want it back. She makes a deal. You give her something, i.e. your soul, and she will bring the world back. If you say No, you will have to wait 10 minutes again for her to offer the deal once more. You can only play the game again if you say Yes.Thank you for reading my guide, and I hope you had fun! Check out the other endings and secrets for more things to do in the game.
