evidences.title: There Is No Game: Wrong Dimension : The Walkthrough King
evidences.text:
There Is No Game: Wrong Dimension : The Walkthrough King
The Walkthrough King
Getting Started
Chapter 1
Chapter 2
Chapter 3
Chapter 4
Chapter 5
Chapter 6
The End
There Is No Game: Wrong Dimension
Game Details:  Comedy, 2021
Steam Achievements:  Completed (34/34)
Links:  Moby Games, Steam
Walkthrough Updated:  8/18/2021
Suggested Listening:  Where There's Smoke (Ash 25)
There Is No Game: Wrong Dimension is a game that doesn't want to be played. Don't trust the narrator, but just explore and discover the hidden game within.
Getting Started
After selecting your language, select the "This Way!" option, followed by Yes It's a trap!. Start the game again, and this time select "No Game Here".
Chapter 1: Mise en Abyme
Curtain Call
Pull the curtain string, then tap the metal plaque repeatedly. Listen to the introduction, then break off both parts of the exclamation mark and bounce the ball around to knock down all of the letters. Tap on the letters of the replacement sign until you are challenged to a game of Roshambo. Choose any 10 cards and you will be beaten every time Roshambo Addict. Now select the paper card, then quickly grab the opponent's scissors. Use the scissors to cut the rope holding up the sign.
Pick up the fallen letter T and drop it repeatedly. Click on the new volume icon, then quickly grab the mouse icon that comes from the right, and drag it up to pop the balloon.
Use the fallen letter T on the 4 screws to remove them. After the door appears, lift up the welcome mat and pick up the key. Try to use this on the lock in the door, but it doesn't fit.
Go left twice and use the key on the French flag padlock so that the flag falls down. Now go right and left quickly until an "Out of memory" message appears. Quickly use the fallen letter T on the gear to make it fall down as well. Go right to the book and flip the pages to the right until you see "Draw me a cog". Use the fallen letter T on the cog here so you have two of them.
Head right and slide the flag beneath the locked door. Use the mouse cursor on the lock, and the narrator will mention a fallen key. Use the mouse cursor on the lock again, then quickly take the flag. Pick up the real key and use it on the lock. When the safe appears, pull out the right side. Insert your 2 cogs on the pegs. Close the right side and spin the wheel. When the progress bar appears, tilt the right side down - you need to repeat this 3 times.
Flying Squirrel OS
Open the music folder and play track 02. With the screen shaking, close the music player and open the private folder. Explore this folder and read all 3 of the truth documents Conspiracy theorist, then open the 18+ folder Have you no shame?. After backing out of these folders, you will receive a phone call from NOTME - answer the call.
Next open the pictures folder and open image 02. Open the drawer in the picture, then turn the picture upside down and a bottle opener will fall out. Use this on the trash can to open it, then look inside Private Detective. Play the pinball game - drag the ball on to the screen and get a score over 1000, then the game will change. Drag the bouncer around to hit the ball until you get a high score. Drag the trophy down to the bottom of the screen, then close the game. Read the other 2 documents in the trash. The password document gives you three hints to the password that is random for each game:
Spot the odd one out on my ID card.
Open image 03 in the pictures folder and find the single digit that is different from the rest.
My favorite number.
Open the sKite app and look at NOTME's profile to find his favorite number.
Miss Tokyo's private number.
Open JP_MISS in the 18+ folder within the private folder and find the number in the picture.
Now open the clock and enter the 6-digit password you have just pieced together Administrator. Once the clock appears, click multiple times on the bottom right of the A in AM to change it to PM. Open the music folder and play track 03. Drag the trophy over the rain to fill the trophy with water. Next open the pictures folder and open image 01. Pour water from your trophy onto the sapling to turn it into a hazelnut tree. Click on this until it drops a hazelnut.
Open the music folder and play track 01. Drag the hazelnut over the nutcracker to break it open. Close the music player and give the nut to the squirrel so that it drops the key. Drag the key over ZinWip twice to shrink it. Use the key on the prohibited folder. Open the folder, then load the program (it will move slower if you keep clicking).
Once the curtain appears, pull the curtain string. Click on Mr Glitch repeatedly in each of the following scenes There's a bug in the soup.
Chapter 2: Behind the Scenes
The Monitor
Tap on the monitor until you can rotate it. Look at the back and plug in the yellow cable. Look at the front again and keep tapping on the glass, then the cracks until the whole screen shatters.
Detective Game
After watching the introduction of the detective game, break off the glass shard from the top of the screen, and use it on the hand icon. Use the fist on the window inside the game. Now you can head out the window. Outside, use the fist on the manhole cover, and Watson will pick up a penny from the ground. A yellow square will appear at the bottom of the interface just before the penny gets added to the inventory; quickly use the glass shard on this yellow square, and the penny will drop out of the game (if you are too slow, just use the fist on the manhole cover again).
Rotate the monitor so you can see the back again, and use the coin on all 4 screws.
You can now see the back of the detective game. Use the fist on the wooden beam on the left. Rotate the monitor to the front again, then go through the open doorway - it is too dark to see anything. Turn to the right side of the monitor and use your glass shard on the peeling gear stamp on the postcard. Turn back to the front of the monitor again. Use the stamp on the tongue icon. Turn back to the rear of the monitor, and use the wet gear stamp on the other gears. Back on the front of the monitor, go back out to the street. Head to the back of the monitor and pull the moon lever. Go to the front and head inside again, where there will now be moonlight visible.
Spin around to the back again and plug in the cable on the left. Also turn the screw visit within the game using your penny. Go back to the front of the monitor, and the eyeball will now be free. Use the fist to punch Wilhelm 7 times and the H will fall off his scream. Punch him another 6 times This is not a Beat'em up.
Within the detective game, head left twice. Spin the monitor around to the back, and use the H on the broken rail tracks. Drag the large backdrop to the right, then use the penny on another screw. Go back to the front of the monitor, and the magnifying glass will now be free. Spin back to the front of the monitor, and use the magnifying glass to look at the hatch in the floor - press the red button to open the hatch, but don't go down yet.
Head right twice and use the magnifying glass on the telephone to the right. Back out and look at the right of the monitor to see a phone number. Go back to the detective game and use the phone to dial the number 555-2368 four times Spam. There is another number that you can dial, which you don't learn about until Chapter 6; there is an achievement for dialing the number 555-8121 now Wrong Dimension.
Go out to the street and pull the moon lever through the back of the monitor, then go back to the game and in to the right - Holmes will move away from the telephone. Repeat this again, and use the telephone to dial the number 555-2368 again. This time Watson will answer the phone. After the conversation, turn around to the back of the monitor again and use the penny on another screw. Go back to the front of the monitor, and the tongue will now be free.
Go out to the street, use the moon lever through the back of the monitor to move the moon again, then go back to the detective game and head inside to the left and down through the open hatch. Spin around to the back of the monitor, and use the tongue on the ice-cream. Unplug the yellow cable from the monitor, then use the cone on the TV to get some TV snow. Plug the yellow cable back in again.
Spin to the front of the monitor again, then go up and spin to the back once more. Use your tongue on the blue paint. Drag the large backdrop to the left, then use your eyeball on the red paint. Go back to the front of the monitor, and head out to the street. Use your blue tongue on the moon Blue moon. Head in to the left again and turn around to the back. Drag the large backdrop to the left, then use your eyeball on the red and green paints. Head back around to the front of the monitor, then go outside and use your yellow tongue on the moon. Turn to the back again and pull the moon lever, then go back around and inside to the right. Use the TV snow on Wilhelm, then add the red eyeball.
After Mr Glitch leaves, use your bug fragment on the painting to unblock the doorway. Head out to the left.
Copy Protection
Part of the way through a conversation in the street, a copy protection system will appear. You can only enter the numbers 1-4, but you can turn the numbers upside down by using the bug fragment on them. If you look at the left side of the monitor, spin the code wheel until the numbers 315 are visible. The two matching symbols at the top are skull and rifle. Go to the back of the monitor and drag the symbols so the skull and rifle are highlighted. Go back to the front and enter the code 315.
After Mr Glitch leaves again, spin around to the back of the monitor and use the bug fragment on the loose cable near the bottom left. Go back to the front of the monitor and look down into the vortex. Return up, left and down. Use the bug fragment on the bathtub, then the fist on the manhole along the right wall. Go through the opening to the right A soundly led quest.
Chapter 3: The Good, the Bad and the Princess
Hero's Home
Press the start button to reveal a new game. Click on the home button until it breaks off and wakes up Hero. Drop the home button again repeatedly from the top of the screen until a chest appears, and Hero retrieves a glitched hammer from it. Use this on anything in the room to realize it shrinks sprites. Quickly use it on every item in the room so they are all small at the same time (bed, barrel, 2 pots, table, chest and dresser) Honey, I blew up the Hero.
With Hero near the dresser, shrink the dresser again to reveal the key, then drop the home button to shake the key down. While Hero is trying to use the key in the door, use the glitched hammer on it.
Verdant Landscape
After Hero stops near the hole, open the inventory by clicking on the bag. Use the glitched hammer on the map, then use the map on the hole. Once you reach the lake, drop the home button down the left of the screen twice, and a chest will land on the bottom lily pad - Hero retrieves a glitched monocle from it.
Use the monocle on the chest and it will enlarge, then sink back into the lake. Now use the glitched monocle on the nearby lily pad. Wait until the lily pad returns to normal size. Now use the monocle on Hero, and the hammer on the lily pad Life sentence.
When you get back to the lake, use the monocle on the bottom lily pad so Hero gets on to it. Now drop the home button on the right until the 2 lily pads are aligned. Use the monocle on both lily pads, and Hero will move to the top one. Drop the home button on the left twice, then use the monocle on the top lily pad again so Hero can reach the other shore.
With Hero trying to free the sword, use the monocle on him, then drop the home button repeatedly until the sword is free. Open the inventory and drop the home button, then put the sword in the skull's mouth to crush the tip of it. Use this on the screen near the life gauge at the top left, then give the sword to Hero.
Once the fight against the guard starts, open the inventory, then use the monocle on the guard, and they will both move to the left - the fight will free the sacred feather. Next use the monocle on the skull, then use the feather on the skull, and the lily pad will be blown down behind the guard. Use the monocle on the lily pad, and the guard will stand on it. Use the monocle on the guard, then drop the home button. Finally, use the monocle on the lily pad, and Hero will go across.
Dimensional Temple
While Hero is carrying the bomb, quickly use the glitched hammer on both barrels and the bomb itself. Once the bomb is in front of the blocked passage, quickly use the glitched monocle on the bomb, and it will clear the way. Inside, with Hero standing on the left switch, drop the home button 5 times so a snake falls on the right switch Raiders of the lost game. Now use the hammer on yourself, the monocle on the snake, and drop the home button once more.
In the darkened room, open the inventory, then drop the home button to the right of the lily pad repeatedly, until the lily pad is beneath the sun fragments icon. Use the glitched monocle on the sun fragments icon, then just hold the monocle over the fire pit on the lily pad (don't actually use it) - this will start a fire. Now drop the home button to the left of the lily pad until it is back near Hero. Use the glitched monocle on the fire to make it larger, and Hero will move right. Gradually move the lily pad right until Hero stands on the moon switch. Move the lily pad back to the right, so Hero returns to the previous room.
There is now a puzzle where you need to activate a set of 4 switches in a specific order. To activate a switch, drop the home button when Hero is over it. The correct order is:
Left
Top
Right
Bottom
There is an achievement for solving this without uncovering any of the hints Random luck!. The hints are obtaining by using the glitched hammer on each of the fires around the room and noticing symbols on the pillars. Hero will grab the boss key and proceed to the next area.
Dark Lord
With Hero running in circles and being chased by the fireball, use the glitched monocle on the fireball and one of the pots. Repeat this for a second fireball. For the third fireball, use the glitched monocle on the fireball and then the Dark Lord.
Now the Dark Lord will start throwing bombs. Use the glitched hammer on each of the first 2 bombs to shrink them. Use the glitched hammer on the bottom bomb the second time, and use the glitched monocle on the top bomb, to damage the Dark Lord.
When the spikes start to advance, drop the home button so Hero jumps over them.
When Hero advances to the Dark Lord, use the glitched hammer on the Dark Lord, and the glitched monocle on Hero. The Dark Lord will eventually be destroyed, but Mr Glitch will come in and ruin things again Princess saver.
Chapter 4: Free2Pay
Hero's Home
Click on Hero and the bed to generate 150 coins, then drag the coin purse on to the alarm clock to wake up Hero. Click on everything to generate coins, and use the coins to buy everything available (furniture, interface, shop items). Make sure you reach the maximum of 1000 coins in your purse. You will eventually need to also pay to leave the house.
Verdant Landscape
Keep clicking on everything to maximize your coins. Click on Hero, then buy the cereal from the shop when prompted. When Hero finds a lootbox, click it to see an advertisement. Quickly drag the spoon over the mound of dirt on the left to clear the path. Keep clicking on the pots and barrel in the meantime, making sure that you again reach the maximum of 1000 coins in your purse. Drop the purse in the hole and Hero will head to the left.
Click on the barrel to once more reach the maximum of 1000 coins in your purse. Around now you should get an achievement for all of your clicking Serial Clicker. Now when Hero finds a lootbox, click it to see an advertisement for Ninecraft. Click on each of the letters to make 9 blocks appear, then drag them down into the 9 slots to show a picture of a pickaxe. With the next lootbox, click it to see an advertisement for Super Cold. Use the pickaxe on the ice man, then drag the ice cubes into the lake to freeze it.
On the next screen, unlock all 4 blades, then give any of them to Hero. Use the vintage blade (straight) on the coin purse, then the snake blade (s-shaped) on the coin purse. Now you can drag this on to the legendary sword in the shop to buy it.
You will end up back at the title screen, needing 3 diamonds to continue. Use your pickaxe on the diamond at the sword hilt, and on the stone in the "of" to find the first 2. Click the continue button, and use the pickaxe on the diamond here. Drag all of the diamonds on to the continue message, then press Start.
Earn coins to wake up Hero again, and you will end up facing the Dark Lord's guard. Unlock all the items here. Lower the bridge and Hero will start fighting, then raise the bridge again. Quickly lower the bridge, and the guard will go on to it. When the fight starts again, raise the bridge to get rid of the guard To infinity and beyond!.
Dimensional Temple
Unlock everything except the bomb here. When Hero finds a lootbox, click it to see an advertisement for RaPappaThePaper. When the rapper is leaning forward and shaking, unlock the bomb, and the rapper should fall out of the advertisement (try again if the timing isn't quite right). With the next lootbox, click it to see an advertisement for Please, Paper! Inside this advertisement, knock on the door and hand over the rapper - you will receive a plunger.
With the next lootbox, use the plunger to stop the arrow on the 1M coin jackpot. Use your prize on the lock and Hero will head inside.
Start by unlocking each of the columns, then unlock the moon switch. Wait until the Crunchy Crunchy Cereal advertisement pops up below, and it will show you the order in which to unlock the switches:
Middle right
Top left
Bottom left
Top right
Unlock the sun switch, then Hero will step on the switches in the same order you unlocked them - the reward will be a stamina gauge boost. Unlock the moon switch again. Wait until the Sudoku advertisement pops up below, and it will show you the order in which to unlock the switches:
Middle left
Bottom right
Top right
Middle right
Unlock the sun switch, then Hero will step on the switches in the same order you unlocked them - the reward will be another stamina gauge boost. Unlock the moon switch again. Wait until the Official Guide advertisement pops up below, and it will show you the order in which to unlock the switches:
Top right
Middle left
Bottom left
Bottom right
Unlock the sun switch, then Hero will step on the switches in the same order you unlocked them - the reward will be another stamina gauge boost, which will knock down the boss key.
In the next room, grab the free gift and drag it in front of Hero to guide him to the top door. Next use the free gift to highlight the switch, then guide Hero to the switch and back to the door. Use the barrels to earn 300 coins, then buy each of the items across the bottom. Use the feather on the skull, then grab the key and use it on the chest to unlock the glitched hammer. Use the glitched hammer on the stamina gauge, and Hero will proceed to the final chamber.
Dark Lord
This time you will automatically kill the Dark Lord One Punch Man.
Once you have the map, go back to the river where you defeated the Dark Lord's guard. Click on the top small bush 5 times as indicated in an advertisement earlier Follow the guide!. Keep exploring until the map is complete, then return to the final screen again. Drag the completed map on to the lock The end of the ordeal.
Chapter 5: Loss of Control
Broken Credits
Wait until Mr Glitch knocks out some of the name plates. Put Buster Chaplin into the slot for backgrounds. Click on the pickaxe beneath the company name to free it. Next move Buster Chaplin into the slot for FX. Use the pickaxe on the chain holding up the plate in the characters slot - this will also turn the pickaxe into a pick. Move Buster Chaplin back into the slot for backgrounds, then use the pick in the bucket of molasses beneath the special thanks section.
Move Buster Chaplin into the slot for characters, then use the molasses-covered pick on the pickaxe head next to the old movie. Now use the pickaxe on the old movie, and the name plate for My Cat will now be free.
Remove Buster Chaplin and insert My Cat into the slot for characters. Take the cat toy from the white cat. Move My Cat into the slot for backgrounds, and Buster Chaplin into the slot for FX. Tear the wallpaper further where it is already partially torn, then use the cat toy on the fish that is revealed. Remove Buster Chaplin, and move My Cat back into the slot for characters. Give the fish to the white cat, and you will end up with fish bones. Put Buster Chaplin into the slot for backgrounds, then use the fish bones on the bucket.
Remove My Cat and insert Buster Chaplin into the slot for characters. Use the empty bucket on the old movie with the water, and you will fill the bucket with water. Remove Buster Chaplin and insert My Cat back into the slot for characters. Use the bucket of water on the sleeping cat A friend of the animals... or not.
Remove all nameplates. Put Tim Elfman into the slot for backgrounds, and the bucket will knock the key out of the creepy door. Next move Tim Elfman into the slot for FX, and My Cat into the slot for backgrounds. Use the empty bucket on the radiator to fill it with water again. Remove My Cat and insert Tim Elfman into the slot for backgrounds. Use the bucket of water in front of the grave, then take the skull by clicking on it multiple times. Put Buster Chaplin into the slot for characters, and My Cat into the slot for FX. Scroll to the old movie of Charlie Chaplin firing a gun, and line the skull up with the red dot so it gets shot Headshot.
Remove Buster Chaplin and insert Tim Elfman into the slot for characters. Give the nut to the creature holding the key, and the key will be stolen by the UFO beneath it. Move Tim Elfman into the slot for backgrounds, and the UFO will return. Add My Cat to the FX slot and Buster Chaplin to the characters slot. Scroll to the old move of Charlie Chaplin again, but this time grab the red dot and drag it over the UFO so it gets shot. You can finally use the key on the creepy door.
Beatboxing
Put My Cat into the game design slot. Grab a fish that is flying around the screen, and use it to lure the cat around the maze. You need to collect all the pellets without getting caught by the cucumber. When you win, grab the gold medal and it will fall down. Use this on the 2 screws, but Mr Glitch will bring in a sturdy lock to replace them.
Next put Tim Elfman into the game design slot. Lose at the laser slice game, and when the UFO comes down, hold the wooden board over its laser so it gets cut into 2 pieces.
Now put Buster Chaplin into the game design slot. You will lose this game quickly, and be able to use your 2 wooden boards on the fallen molasses that appears on the screen briefly.
Put Tim Elfman back into the game design slot. Attach your sticky boards to the game controls, then play Tetris until the door opens. Grab the key and use it on the lock. Mr Glitch will now erect a brick wall.
Return Buster Chaplin to the game design slot. You can now drop the metal block to make the game character jump over obstacles. Fail this game multiple times to eventually be given an easy mode with a single obstacle. Beat this easy mode Serge Karamasov. Grab the GG name plate. Put GG into the game design slot.
Card Games
Once you have a spinning circle of 7 cards, just use trial and error to uncover in ascending order from 1-7. Now you need to listen to the chorus of the song and place six cards in order to create a program. From left to right these are:
No color
Cry
End
Game
Loop
Home
Once they are in position, press the Return button. There is an achievement for getting this correct on your first try Carmack junior. Watch the final scenes of this chapter It's not over yet.
Chapter 6: Back Home
Love Game
Pull the curtain string, then just listen to the introduction. When the wind starts, catch the language flag, gear, and goat. The water level will rise, so you can untie the string holding the scissors. Insert the 6 letters on the board that are floating in the water. Use the scissors to cut the L off the lower banner, and insert this as well. Repeat this with the V from the upper banner.
When given the option, choose to quit, then select Yes followed by No. Repeat this multiple times, each time clicking on all the Yes options before finally clicking on No Skeet shoot.
Rogue Quiz
Answer the quiz questions as follows:
4
Paris
Instant cassettes Better than Mr. Radar
Breakdown
Back at the title screen, select Chapter 1, then pull the curtain string and repeatedly tap the panel. Pull up the bottom right corner of the screen when it starts to peel. Once the door floats all the way down, go to the Pause menu and grab the key Outside the box.
Resume the game and try to use the key on the door, but it will be trapped inside a bubble. Bounce the bubble down towards the bottom left. Click on the audio icon, so that when the mouse pointer zooms in it pops the bubble. Now click on the audio icon again, then use the key on the lock when the bubbles temporarily stop coming.
Retro Phone
Click on the lock icon to reveal a 3x3 grid, then click on the ? button for a clue. You need to spell out LOL as follows:
Now drag the 3 letters up on to the key outline, then use the key in the lock. On the new interface, select Call, then dial the number from Chapter 2 (555-2368) Who you gonna call?. Read all the messages in the Inbox (this is where you learn the number that you dialed for another achievement back in Chapter 2). Next read all the messages in the Outbox and click on the attachments in this order:
PATCH_03
PATCH_02
PATCH_01
Go back to the first screen of the phone and go right to the second page of apps, then start the RPG_GPS application and select to start navigation. Go back to the phone and call 555-8121 repeatedly, until the creator doesn't even answer any more. Go to the second page of apps and start the Home application. Enter any code, then select the reset code option. Back out and check your email to get a new code, then login to the Home application with this code.
First set the temperature as low as possible. Next prepare a coffee; it should be espresso, sugar-free and strong. Repeat this several times, until the creator gets zapped by the router. Answer the call from Mr Glitch. Return to the Home application and set the lighting to boost mode, then wait for another phone call and answer it. Now use the Home application to set the temperature as high as possible. Use the phone to dial 555-8121 once more.
Desktop Computer
Tap on the screen. Close most of the windows, leaving Virtual Beat Coin open - eventually the coin on this app will start flashing. Now close windows to also leave Super Fireshot IV open. Drag the coin into the slot.
Now close windows until you have Super Fireshot IV and Bombsweeper open. Play the Super Fireshot IV game until your ship has a flamethrower. Quickly lose at the Bombsweeper game, then drag the large bomb from here on to your flamethrower.
Once the tic-tac-toe game starts, grab a symbol when it turns pink, and drop it on the progress bar. Repeat this 2 more times. The next time, grab a symbol and drop it on to the lines at the bottom right. Now controlling these, bounce an O up to the progress bar again. Repeat this 2 more times.
In the next sequence you just have to keep grabbing Mr Glitch so that the entire command can be typed successfully Home at last.
The End: Epilog
Pull the curtain string, then pull the large chain on the left 3 times. Use the screwdriver on the 4 screws. Now there are 2 buttons to press. Choose one, then load the Epilog and choose the other:
Press the top button, then start the adventure I want to PLAY!!
Press the bottom button Future of the planet, and all that...
These walkthroughs may not be published or reproduced in any format without the author's prior written consent. All game titles, art and character references remain the copyright of their respective holders. View our Privacy Policy.

CategoriesHobbies and CraftsGamesVideo Games"There Is No Game" Walkthrough: Easy Step-by-Step Guide
Download Article
Defeat the flying squirrel, free the goat & solve other tricky puzzles
Co-authored by
Elaine Heredia, BA
Last Updated: February 28, 2025
Fact Checked
Download Article
There Is No Game Walkthrough
|How long does it take to beat it?
|Where to Play
|FAQs
|Video
|Q&A
|Show more
|Show less
X
This article was co-authored by wikiHow staff writer, Elaine Heredia, BA. Elaine Heredia is a staff writer at wikiHow. Elaine graduated with a B.A. in English from Texas Tech University in 2017. Since 2020, she has been writing articles on a wide variety of topics for a diverse range of clients, from business thought leaders to marketers to hobby shop owners. Elaine now writes and edits content at wikiHow, where her goal is to craft useful, enjoyable articles that answer readers’ most pressing queries. She enjoys expanding her knowledge alongside wikiHow readers and adding new topics of interest to her writing quiver.
There are 15 references cited in this article, which can be found at the bottom of the page.
This article has been fact-checked, ensuring the accuracy of any cited facts and confirming the authority of its sources.
This article has been viewed 11,171 times.
Learn more...
There Is No Game is a computer game that claims to not be a game, but that's not actually true! Not only can you play it, but you can also beat it – you just have to know where to click on the screen and what objects to move around. This easy, step-by-step walkthrough will give you all the guidance you need to make it through every phase of There Is No Game, from the title screen to the Pong game to the goat rescue challenge!
How to Play There Is No Game
To beat There Is No Game, start by solving the puzzle on the title screen by spelling "GAME" on the letter board. Beat the Pong game by breaking the blocks to collect the trophy. Then, find a goat, grow a tree, and get the key from the squirrel to release the goat from its cage.
Steps
Section 1 of 4:
There Is No Game Walkthrough
Download Article
1
Solve the puzzle on the There Is No Game title screen. What makes this game so unique (and so confusing!) is the first puzzle in the game is actually the title screen itself. Players can figure out how to solve it by themselves simply by moving their mouse around and clicking on letters and buttons until something happens. The narrator gives you clues on what you should do next to progress. But if you want to cut to the chase and solve the title screen quickly, follow these steps.[1]
X
Research source
On the title screen, click the "O" in "NO" 10 times to make it fall. Then click and drag the "O" back up to the title to try to put it back in place. The letter will fall back down every time you let go of it and "shake" the screen. Repeat this step several times until a large volume button falls from the top right corner of the screen.[2]
X
Research source
Click on the volume button to mute the narrator. Next, the volume button will start jumping quickly to different parts of the screen. Follow it with your mouse and click it again. Then the narrator will trap the button in a wooden box, which you can break by dragging the "O" over it and dropping it. The narrator will then try trapping the volume button in a metal box. Pick up and drop the metal box with your mouse to "shake" the screen and cause all the letters in the title to fall down.[3]
X
Research source
With the letters on the ground, all that will be left in the middle of the screen is 2 screws. Unscrew them by left-clicking on them with your mouse. A panel will open on the screen and reveal a letter board that reads "THERE IS A” with 4 blank spaces after it. Drag and drop the letters that spell "GAME" up to the blank spaces on the letter board.[4]
X
Research source
2
Beat the Pong game and collect the trophy. After you beat the first title screen, the screen changes to a game of Pong with several rows of blocks. Underneath the blocks, you'll notice a horizontal bar with a ball stuck to it. Click and drag it around with your mouse to release the ball and bounce it up into the blocks. Each time the ball touches a block, it breaks it. Your goal is to break each block without letting the ball fall past the bar. Of course, since this is There Is No Game, there's a clever twist! Here's how to overcome it.
Drag the bar around the screen to destroy as many blocks as you can with the ball. After a while, the narrator will try to make it more difficult by moving the remaining blocks around. As you continue to break more blocks, the narrator will get frustrated and delete your ball.[5]
X
Research source
Replace the ball by dragging the ball from underneath your score on the left side of the screen. Let go of it so it bounces off the horizontal bar or the bottom of the screen, then destroy more blocks. Flashing colored blocks will appear on either side of the Pong screen as the game "glitches," but don't let that distract from your objective: eliminate all the blocks in the middle.[6]
X
Research source
When all the blocks are destroyed, a gleaming trophy will appear in the middle of the screen. Click on it to make it drop, then pick it up and drag it over to the blinking green arrow on the right side of the screen. You'll be taken back to the title screen, which is where you'll start the next puzzle.[7]
X
Research source
Advertisement
3
Find the goat and grow a tree to help you chase the flying squirrel. During the last stage of the Pong game, the narrator brainstorms things he thinks will make the game (which he keeps insisting isn't a real game at all) better. The last item he mentions is a goat, and this is an extremely important clue to keep in mind as you continue to progress. Here's how to find the goat and use it to advance.
When you return to the title screen after beating the Pong game, drop the trophy. This will cause the screen to "shake" and the letters on the letter board to fall on the ground. Pick up the letters that spell "GOAT" and arrange them on the letter board, then click the green arrow that appears on the right side of the screen.[8]
X
Research source
The arrow will take you to a new screen where you'll see a goat locked in a cage that's dangling from the ceiling. You'll also notice a waterfall to the goat's right. Nothing will happen if you click around, so your only alternative is to click the green arrow on the left to return to the title screen.[9]
X
Research source
After returning to the title screen, a flying squirrel with a key will appear and float to the top of the screen. The key is for the goat's cage, so you'll need to get it! To do that, replace the letters that spell "GOAT" on the letter board with the letters that spell "TREE." A tree will appear on the ground.[10]
X
Research source
Pick up the trophy and hover on the right-hand green arrow to go back to the cave where the goat is still trapped in its cage. Hold the trophy underneath the waterfall to fill it up with water. Then drag it back to the title screen and drop it above the tree. Repeat this process 3 times to make the tree grow tall enough that you can use it to follow the squirrel.[11]
X
Research source
4
Get the key and free the goat from its cage. When the top of the tree grows to reach the top of the screen, a new green arrow will appear next to it pointing upward. Click on it to change the screen to a sunny day with a blue sky, the top of the tree, and the pesky squirrel with the cage key perched on its leaves. Here's how you can persuade it to give up the key in exchange for a nut.
Find a brown nut on the right side of the tree top and click it until it falls down to the title screen. Follow it down, then click on the metal box from the first puzzle, drag it over the top of the nut, and drop it. The box will crack the nut's shell.[12]
X
Research source
Pick up and drag the shelled nut up to the screen with the squirrel and give it to the squirrel by holding it over his face. The squirrel will take the nut and drop the key down on to the title screen.[13]
X
Research source
Pick up and drag the key to the right to go back to the screen with the goat. Hold the key over the goat's cage. The cage will unlock and the goat will drop to the ground – but then, the screen will "glitch," and the narrator will scream, "No… the goat was a lie! A lie!!" Eventually, the screen will turn white, then fade to black.[14]
X
Research source
5
End the game by choosing if you want to forgive the narrator. After the goat screen fades to black, the narrator returns! He happily exclaims, "I'm not dead! I'm alive!" and then immediately expresses how he regrets glitching the game and putting you (the player) in danger. He asks you if you'll forgive him. Saying either "Yes" or "No" will end the game, but the narrator's dialogue changes depending on your response.[15]
X
Research source
If you say "No": The narrator will reply, "Go to hell!" Then the screen will, "Not a Game by Kamizoto."
If you say "Yes": The narrator will say, "Ho?! Really!? What a great news! Maybe we can be friends! No, no, that's not a good idea. We would get bored quickly together. Why? Because… as I said… THERE IS NO GAME!!!" Then the text changes to read, "Not a Game by Kamizoto."
Advertisement
Section 2 of 4:
How long does it take to beat There Is No Game?
Download Article
Players can beat the game in less than 10 minutes. If you know where and what to click on, you can finish the game in almost no time flat! If you decide not to use our walkthrough as a reference, though, it could take you 2-4 hours to figure out what to do to solve each puzzle! Both ways of playing the game are valid so it's up to you to decide which one sounds the most fun to you.
Section 3 of 4:
Where to Play There Is No Game
Download Article
1
Play There Is No Game for free online. You don't have to pay a cent for it, nor do you have to download any programs. The entire game is playable right from your web browser! Find it on Poki, CoolMathGames, or CrazyGames.
2
Buy the There Is No Game sequel on PC, Switch, or Android. If you beat There Is No Game but feel hungry for more, try out the sequel, There Is No Game: Wrong Dimension. Unlike the first installment, however, you do have to pay for WD. It costs $12.99 and is available for purchase on Steam and Nintendo Switch. You can also buy the app version from the Google Play Store for $4.99.
Advertisement
Section 4 of 4:
Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)
Download Article
1
What do you do with the goat in There Is No Game? The only thing you can do with the goat in There Is No Game is free it with the key you get from the flying squirrel. After you unlock the goat's cage, it drops to the ground, but freeing it is the only interaction you have with it.
2
How do you beat the squirrel in There Is No Game? To beat the squirrel, click on the nut on the right side of the tree where the squirrel is sitting several times until it falls. Follow it down to the title screen and break open the nut's shell by dropping the metal box on it. Pick up the shelled nut and carry it back up to the squirrel. The squirrel will take the nut and drop the key.
3
How do you crack the nut in There Is No Game? Crack the nut in There Is No Game by picking up the metal box from the first puzzle with your mouse and dropping it on the nut.
4
What's the end to There Is No Game? At the end of There Is No Game, the narrator glitches the game to try to end it. When the screen goes black, the narrator is surprised to find he's still alive. Then he apologizes to the player for putting them in danger and asks for their forgiveness. The player can either say "Yes" or "No." If they say "Yes," the narrator speaks a few more lines of dialogue and then the game ends. If they "No," the narrator says, "Go to hell!" and the game ends.
Advertisement
Community Q&A
Search
Add New Question
Question
Is there more than one ending?
WikiWombatWatcher392
Community Answer
No, there are two endings. You can choose to forgive or not, and forgiving results in the narrator saying more lines of dialogue.

evidences.title: How to enable PS5 Console Sharing and Offline Play
evidences.text: How to enable PS5 Console Sharing and Offline Play  Support Home PSN StatusAccount & SecurityPS Store & RefundsSubscriptionsGamesHardware & RepairsOnline SafetyConnectivityPC How to enable PS5 Console Sharing and Offline PlayFind out how to enable Console Sharing and Offline Play on PlayStation®5 consoles to share your purchases and some subscription benefits with other players.Turn on console sharingTurn off console sharingConsole sharing featuresActivation limitsRemote deactivationHaving issues?For information about activating a ‎PlayStation®4 console, visit the guide below. Activate a primary PS4 console How to enable PS5 Console Sharing and Offline PlayConsole Sharing and Offline Play is automatically enabled on the PS5® console you've used to sign in to PlayStation™Network for the first time. To enable Console Sharing and Offline Play on a new PS5 console, follow these steps: Go to Settings > Users and Accounts > Other > Console Sharing and Offline Play. Select Enable or Disable. If you have another PS5 console enabled, disable your previous console to activate a new PS5 console. Deactivate a console If you want to disable a console you no longer have access to, you can remotely deactivate all devices.What is PS5 Console Sharing and Offline Play? When you enable Console Sharing and Offline Play, anyone who uses your PS5 console can:Play your games and media even when the console is offline.Play games and media you've purchased and downloaded.Enjoy some benefits of your PlayStation®Plus membership. Share PlayStation Plus benefits PS5 Console Sharing and Offline Play limits You can enable Console Sharing and Offline Play on one PS5 console at a time.PS5 Console Sharing and Offline Play does not affect your other console activation limits. PlayStation console activation limits How to remotely deactivate PS5 console sharing To deactivate a console you no longer have access to, sign in to Account Management and deactivate all devices. Please note, you can only remotely deactivate all devices every 6 months. Remotely deactivate all consoles Having issues? Can't enable console sharing and offline play?If you have Console Sharing and Offline Play enabled on another PS5 console, disable the console to change activation. Disable a console Check the PSN status page. If you see any alerts, wait until the service is up and running and then try again. PSN Status Check that your console has the latest system software installed. Update PS5 console system software Turn off your router and wait at least 5 minutes, then restart your router and try again.If you're experiencing connection issues, find connection solutions for your console below. PS Repairs Restart your console.Can't find or access content or benefitsMake sure the user who enabled Console Sharing and Offline Play purchased the content. Check transaction history If the game or benefit is included as part of your PlayStation®Plus subscription, make sure the subscription is active. If you're looking for a PlayStation Plus game, check that it's still available by visiting the PlayStation Plus guide. PlayStation Plus guide If the game is owned by someone else who shares the console, make sure they have enabled Console Sharing and Offline Play.If you're seeing a padlock icon next to a game, it could be because of license or network issues. Check the guide below for troubleshooting steps. Padlock icons in games Was this article helpful? Smile Frown What went wrong?  The article doesn’t answer my question.  I find the article confusing.  The article has outdated information. Thank you! Your feedback will help us to keep improving. Question About Owning Multiple Playstation 5 Consoles
Thread starter
striderno9
Start date
Mar 10, 2023
Discussion
Gaming Forum

#1
I recently bought a second PS5 to keep at my partner's house. She's going to make a free PSN account and will make a profile on the console. I'd like for her to have access to all my digital games, including PS+. This is still possible on PlayStation consoles, right? Of course, I'll have my main primary PS5 at my home.
Deleted member 93062
Account closed at user request
Banned
Mar 4, 2021
24,767
Mar 10, 2023
#2
Make the PS5 at her house, your account's main console/enable console sharing. The PS5 at your house will need to be connected to internet to access your games though.
How to enable PS5 Console Sharing and Offline Play
Find out how to enable Console Sharing and Offline Play on ‎PlayStation®5 consoles to share your purchases and some subscription benefits with other players.
www.playstation.com

I think it works like it did on PS4, you will need to set the PS5 at your partners home to be your primary PS5. Then your partner should be able to access all of your games. At your home your ps5 will be a secondary system and as long as your are logged into PSN you should be able to access all of your games.

The benefit of enabling sharing is the other users on the PS5 can play your digital games. Enable sharing from your account on her PS5 so she can access your digital games. She will need to sign in to your account to download your digital games, then switch back to her own account to play them. You won't need sharing enabled on your PS5 because you'll be signed into your account, which owns the games.

The whole point of setting a primary console is so your partner can access your games while logged in as them while on "secondary" consoles, you have to be logged in to access your stuff. This typically isn't an issue because why would you be logged in as a different user on your own console? I did this with my dad so he can access all my vr games on ps4. I set his console as "primary" so he can access those games regardless of which profile is logged in (i.e. his) while I can always access my games on my console because I'm logged in as me, the actual account holder.

evidences.title: How to deactivate a PlayStation console
evidences.text: How to deactivate a PlayStation console  Support Home PSN StatusAccount & SecurityPS Store & RefundsSubscriptionsGamesHardware & RepairsOnline SafetyConnectivityPC How to deactivate a PlayStation consoleLearn how to deactivate your PlayStation consoles to allow new console activation and avoid activation limits.Deactivate a consoleRemotely deactivate a consoleActivation limitsActivate a consoleHow to deactivate a PS5 consoleTo activate a new PlayStation®5 console, you need to manually deactivate your previous PS5® console. If you can't access the console, it's still possible to remotely deactivate all devices attached to your account. Go to Settings > Users and Accounts > Other. Select Console Sharing and Offline Play > Disable.Please note, deactivating your console does not delete your data or damage your console.PS4 console: deactivate primary consoleGo to Settings > Account Management > Activate as Your Primary PS4.Select Deactivate > Yes.PS3 console: deactivate consoleSelect your local user account and go to PlayStation Network > Sign-in > Account Management.Go to System Activation and highlight PS3.Select the type of content you want to deactivate for your PlayStation®3 console: Game or Video.Select Deactivate System.PS Vita/TV: deactivate systemTap Settings on the home screen.Go to PlayStation Network > System Activation.Select the type of content you want to deactivate: Game or Video.Tap Deactivate > OK.How to remotely deactivate all consoles  If you can't access a PlayStation console, it’s still possible to deactivate and sign out of all devices attached to your account.You can only deactivate your consoles once every six months.Sign in to Account Management.Select Device Management > PlayStation Consoles > Deactivate All Devices.You can’t remotely deactivate individual PlayStation consoles. If you want to deactivate inaccessible consoles, you need to deactivate all consoles.To sign out from inaccessible consoles, change your password. Reset password Important: activation limits Activating a console links it to your account and allows you to access PlayStation™Store purchases. You can connect your account to a limited amount of consoles. You can have the following activations on your account:Type of ConsoleActivation LimitsPS5 console*1PlayStation®4 console1PlayStation®3 console2PlayStation® Vita/TV3* Console sharing and Offline PlayYou can have both a PS5 console and a PS4™ console activated at the same time.If you use your account on multiple PS5 consoles or PS4 consoles, you can only activate one each.How to activate a PlayStation console Activating your console allows you to share purchases and certain subscription benefits with other players on that console.    Activate PS5 Console Sharing and Offline Play  Activate PS4 consoles, PS3 consoles and PS Vita Was this article helpful? Smile Frown What went wrong?  The article doesn’t answer my question.  I find the article confusing.  The article has outdated information. Thank you! Your feedback will help us to keep improving. Posted bypulmoll26#9311on Dec 5, 2024, 4:10:03 PMQuote this Post
playstation access key isnt available at moment, and you should note that playstation can only use Key from the pack from playstation and not from xbox or any PC platform. playstation packs should be available in the next 24hr thought.
to be clear you need to buy a separate playstion key from playstation to get EA, buying from steam doesnt give EA to playstation users

Playstation Supporter Packs will go live in about 4½ hours from now.

"
pulmoll26#9311 wrote:
Good luck friends, how can someone with an early access key activate the key on the PS5 console? What needs to be done? Can you help?
If you got your key via messages/e-mail because you spend $480 or more like I did, just go on the PS store on your Playstation, top right of the store are 3 dots. Click on those and choose the Redeem Code option to enter your Access key. It should show up as Path of Exile 2, confirm the claim and you're ready to download the game.

Usama Ali
Published Dec 27, 2023

Bitlife stands out as one of the best life sims available on mobile platforms. Unlike many mobile games, it offers players the chance to live a new life in whatever way they want to. Players have complete independence over shaping the lives of their characters. Furthermore, players are allowed to choose a career as their character grows. Various professions are available to suit the tastes of different players. However, those players who want to cut the legwork out of the equation and notch up some quick hard cash can opt to live a criminal’s life.
Related
BitLife: How to Complete Ultimate Bully Challenge
This guide will help players complete BitLife's Ultimate Bully Challenge by detailing how to cause mischief, how to perform a prank, and more.
Posts
How to Rob a Train
If players want to step into a criminal's shoes, they're presented with a range of options. From pickpocketing a stranger to killing someone, there are a variety of activities to get engaged in. Among these options, robbing a train is an activity that many players long for. However, executing this successfully might prove to be a challenge for those unfamiliar with the process. An essential point to note is that characters must be at least 18 years old to execute the train robbery. Yet, if your character is not an adult, it is possible to age swiftly by tapping on the “+ Age” button at the center of the screen. While doing so, players will be presented with various scenarios where their choices influence the future traits and circumstances of their characters. Thus, being mindful of these decisions is crucial. The option to Rob a Train can be accessed once your character is at least 18 years old. However, it is crucial to remember that this is one of the most complicated crimes within the game due to a particular catch. Players should ensure that the in-game time aligns with the time zone they're playing in. Here are the steps for successfully carrying out the train robbery in Bitlife.
Tap the "Activity" option located on the bottom right of the screen.
From the available activities, choose “Crime.”
Scroll down and tap on the “Rob a Train” option.
Following that, players need to select both the train and the time for the robbery. As mentioned earlier, it's essential to ensure that the planned time for the heist aligns with the player's time zone. The following table provides detailed information regarding the actual times.
Sunrise
6 am
High Noon
12 pm
4:20 pm
4:20 pm
Sunset
6 pm
Midnight
11 am
Once the time of the robbery and the player’s time zone align, they’ll be able to successfully pull off the robbery and get their hands on $5-$30 million. Or else the message “Unexpected Delays” will show up.
If players try changing the time on their phones just for robbery, Bitlife will catch it, and the character will end up dead!

CategoriesHobbies and CraftsGamesVideo GamesHow to Rob a Train in BitLife: Guide, Achievements, and Secrets
Download Article
How to complete a BitLife train robbery the first time, every time
Written by
Hannah Dillon
Last Updated: March 10, 2025
Fact Checked
Download Article
Robbing a Train
|Achievements & Secrets
|Video
|Q&A
|Show more
|Show less
X
This article was co-authored by wikiHow staff writer, Hannah Dillon. Hannah Dillon is a Technology Writer and Editor at wikiHow. She graduated with a B.A. in Journalism from North Dakota State University in 2013 and has since worked in the video game industry as well as a few newspapers. From a young age Hannah has cultivated a love for writing and technology, and hopes to use these passions in tandem to help others in the articles she writes for wikiHow.
This article has been fact-checked, ensuring the accuracy of any cited facts and confirming the authority of its sources.
This article has been viewed 63,520 times.
Learn more...
If you're an achievement hunter in BitLife, you may be wondering how you're supposed to rob a train in the game. Robbing the midnight train is how you get the "Going Anywhere" achievement, but any time you try to rob the train the game tells you that you missed it. Thankfully, robbing a train in BitLife isn't difficult, it just requires some patience, as you must try to rob the train at certain precise times in your timezone. Keep reading to learn more about robbing a train in BitLife and what achievements or secrets you can get from doing so.
Things You Should Know
To rob a train in Bitlife, you must try to rob it at the exact time that the app says, but in your timezone.
So, to get the achievement for robbing a midnight train, you must rob the train when it's actually midnight.
You must have a character over 18 to do this, and cheating by changing your phone's time will result in character death.
Steps
Section 1 of 2:
Robbing a Train
Download Article
1
Start with a character who is age 18 or older. You can either start a brand new game in BitLife, or you can do this with a pre-existing character. The only prerequisite is that your character is 18 or older.[1]
X
Research source
2
Tap the Activities menu, then go to Crime. The option to rob a train is under the Crime menu in BitLife, at the bottom of the page.[2]
X
Research source
Advertisement
3
Tap Train Robbery. There will be an option to select a train line and a time of day. The train line isn't important, but the time of day is very important: the time of day must align to the current time in your timezone.[3]
X
Research source
If you try to rob a train at a time that doesn't match what your current time is, you'll miss the train.
The time selection and corresponding actual times are as follows:
Sunrise - 6 a.m.
High Noon - 12 p.m.
4:20PM - 4:20 p.m.
Sunset - 6 p.m.
Midnight - 12 a.m.
4
Set an alarm a few minutes before you need to rob the train. This is especially important if the next train isn't for a few hours, so you don't forget. For example, if you want to rob the sunset train, set an alarm for 5:58 p.m. to allow yourself enough time to open the app back up and get ready.
Once your alarm goes off, go back to the train robbery menu and get your train line and train time selected in the dropdowns, but don't push Rob it until you're ready.
5
Press the Rob it button right as the time changes. If you did it correctly your character will rob the train and will likely get away with a huge sum of cash (over $5 million and potentially up to $30 million).[4]
X
Research source
If you didn't get the right time (i.e. clicked the button early, waited too long) you'll get a popup message stating that you failed, but you can always try again when the timing is correct.
Don't change the time on your phone to get around having to wait. If you attempt this, BitLife can tell that you've done it and your character will die when they try to rob the train.
Advertisement
Section 2 of 2:
Achievements & Secrets
Download Article
1
Get the "Going Anywhere" achievement by robbing a midnight train. There are no extra steps to completing this achievement, you just need to rob a train at midnight. This will require staying up late, since you can't change the time on your phone to get around the timing aspect.[5]
X
Research source
Setting an alarm may be crucial for this one if you're not usually a night owl.
2
Get the "Bandit" death ribbon for committing a train robbery during your life. This is a secret ribbon, and it's not easy to get unless you're specifically trying to get it. The reason this ribbon is a bit more difficult to get is because you can get other ribbons instead, even if you've committed a train robbery in your character's life.[6]
X
Research source
The easiest way to get this ribbon is to try and get it specifically. Start any random life in BitLife, then age your character to 18. Don't do anything else of significance in this life, to avoid accidentally getting another ribbon instead.
Once your character is 18, rob a train once. You'll need to follow the steps in this article to successfully rob a train.
After you've robbed the train, have your character die before they do anything else noteworthy. There are a number of ways to do this, but an easy one is to die by changing the time on your phone and trying to rob a train again. This will result in the death of your character, and you should get the Bandit ribbon easily.
Advertisement
Community Q&A
Search
Add New Question
Question
Why? Just why would you waste your time on this?
Gabe Fletcher
Community Answer
The objective of the game is to get all the ribbons, and this is one of the ribbons. Why do you buy Robux or V-Bucks or MineCoins with your parent's credit card? "For cosmetic purposes obviously!" Well, why do you do that, even if it's such a useless part of the game? Just because a certain part of a game isn't fun for you, or instantly gratifying, doesn't mean it isn't fun for everyone.

FeaturesShowcase‘BitLife’ Ruined My Real Life: A Dangerous Mobile App ExposedChristinna LongeneckerWingspan writer Jon’nette Kidd descends into the trappings one of this year’s most addictive mobile games.  Jon'nette Kidd, Copy EditorMarch 29, 2019 In a time full of emotional and political travesties not only in America, but all over the world, people are grasping at whatever sort of control they can gain. Whether it be cleaning your room, or dyeing your hair – we’re all just trying to have at least a semblance of order within our lives.
Now, enter BitLife, a life simulation mobile game which allows you to customize, randomize, and memorialize your “Bit-lives”. BitLife is a game where you have a randomized name and character, and are expected to live this new life to the fullest. The actions you make very much impact your life, and sometimes will choose whether you live or die. (…In the game, of course.)
The game is sweeping across the world as one of the most addicting apps, at least in my opinion. Once you’ve started your first life, you never want to stop.
Because the real world is so hectic and unexpected, people (like me) turn to whatever substitute money can buy (in this case a $1.99 Bitizenship subscription). According to my phone’s screen time I spend over an hour and a half playing BitLife a day. I know what you might be thinking, “An hour and a half is not a lot.”, and it’s not – but to just stare at your phone for multiple hours every day and play a monotonous game nonstop with no real end goal is truly alarming to  think about.
I still remember the first time I ever played BitLife. It was seventh period World Cultures, and I didn’t have an iPhone, but my friend did (it was only available on IOS at the time). So naturally, I was snooping on her phone, taking pictures, playing games – when suddenly I stumbled upon a bright red app with a sperm cell on it.
An internal war raged in my mind as I struggled between paying attention in class and playing this seemingly interesting game. I was weak, and the red on the cover was so bright.
I opened the app.
I was greeted by a “life” my friend must have never finished and I tentatively asked, “Can I play it?” She said yes. It was all downhill from there.
When I finally got an iPhone, a deep ridden euphoria coursed through my veins and my fingers were lightning fast as I downloaded BitLife.
I immediately played it for 3 hours straight.
For days I spent playing life after life, quickly becoming more obsessed with the interactive game and itching for another fix, and even growing irritable if I didn’t play it for more than a day.
At the time, BitLife was my favorite (and only) pastime. Each night carried dreams of the game, at breakfast my fingers itched to tap on a screen. Fantasies of new lives spiraling around in my head- all I wanted to do was play this game.
Classes were failed (Okay, maybe I just missed one or two assignments). Friendships, lost (Okay, so I might have yelled at my friend in seventh period for not letting me play.) At one point, I had become so involved in this game that no one could tear me away from my phone.
At one point, an intervention attempt was made. My friends took my phone and explained that if I wanted to keep my grades up, I would stop playing this game – but my love of BitLife outweighed the love of my friends and family.
I didn’t play the game for three days.
THREE DAYS? You might ask. Appalling, I know, but truly all motivation to play the game was lost, I just found myself uninterested. Even the mere mention of the game had me annoyed, and without explanation, it seemed like having the opportunity to play the game all the time resulted in my depreciation for the game.
I began to hate it and I didn’t even have insight on why.
A reverse withdrawal, the doctor called it, but it felt like I was a motionless zombie coasting through life. BitLife no longer excited me, and nothing had made me as happy prior to, so what was left for me?
After a while of this meandering, I decided to give it another chance. At this point, I hadn’t played the game in a week and a half, and I was desperate for the relief that it may bring.
It wasn’t long before I fell back into the downward spiral. This time it was worse than before. I played it every chance I got. I woke up in the middle of the night, sweating and yearning to check my phone just so I could make sure that my Bit-life was going well. At one point, I had a dream that my virtual character died without my knowledge. Horrifying.
I asked around to see if other people experienced an obsession with the app as well, and while nobody’s experience was as extreme as mine, a lot of people agreed that it is a very addicting game. It is currently right next to Cat Condo as being one of the most entertaining apps in my opinion, BitLife brings an entirely new aspect to daily gaming for teenagers.
#BitLife#cellphones#humor#mobilegames

Title: The Perils of BitLife: How a Mobile App Disrupted Reality
In the age of ubiquitous mobile apps, few have stirred controversy as profoundly as BitLife. What began innocuously as a life simulation game quickly morphed into a societal phenomenon, blurring the lines between virtual and actual existence. Despite its initial allure as a mere pastime, BitLife has insidiously seeped into the fabric of everyday life, leaving a trail of disrupted realities in its wake.
First and foremost, BitLife’s addictive nature cannot be overstated. Designed to simulate the entirety of a person’s life, the game draws players into a virtual realm where they can make decisions spanning from birth to death. The allure lies in the freedom to live out countless alternate lives, each with its unique set of choices and consequences. However, this freedom quickly transforms into an obsession for many users, who find themselves constantly tethered to their devices, compulsively navigating the intricacies of their digital personas.
Moreover, BitLife’s influence extends beyond mere entertainment, infiltrating psychological and emotional well-being. As players immerse themselves in the game’s virtual universe, they often become emotionally invested in the fate of their digital avatars. The highs and lows experienced within the game can evoke real-world emotions, blurring the boundaries between fiction and reality. Consequently, individuals may find themselves grappling with feelings of anxiety, depression, or inadequacy as they struggle to reconcile their virtual achievements with their actual lives.
The impact of BitLife is particularly pronounced among impressionable youth, who may lack the cognitive maturity to discern between fantasy and reality. With its simplistic interface and seemingly harmless premise, the game appeals to a wide demographic, including minors. However, prolonged exposure to BitLife’s morally ambiguous scenarios can desensitize young minds to the gravity of real-life decisions, fostering a skewed sense of morality and ethical responsibility.
Furthermore, BitLife’s depiction of social interactions and relationships can perpetuate harmful stereotypes and unrealistic expectations. In the pursuit of entertainment value, the game often sensationalizes themes such as romance, friendship, and family dynamics. Consequently, impressionable players may internalize these portrayals, adopting skewed perceptions of healthy relationships and interpersonal dynamics, which can have detrimental effects on their real-world interactions.
The insidious nature of BitLife lies in its ability to normalize and trivialize complex societal issues. By reducing life’s multifaceted complexities into a series of binary choices, the game oversimplifies real-world dilemmas, diminishing their gravity and nuance. Issues such as addiction, mental health, and socioeconomic disparities are distilled into mere gameplay mechanics, devoid of the profound implications they hold in reality.
BitLife’s impact on productivity and personal development cannot be overlooked. As players invest countless hours navigating their digital existences, they often neglect real-world responsibilities and opportunities for growth. The instant gratification provided by the game’s simulated experiences may deter individuals from pursuing meaningful endeavors or confronting real-life challenges, perpetuating a cycle of stagnation and escapism.
Moreover, BitLife’s monetization strategies exacerbate its detrimental effects, particularly among vulnerable demographics. While the game itself is free to download, it employs various tactics to entice players into making in-app purchases, such as virtual currency and premium features. This predatory approach preys on impulsive spending behaviors, ensnaring users in a cycle of financial exploitation that further exacerbates the negative consequences of excessive gaming.
In conclusion, BitLife stands as a cautionary tale of the potential pitfalls inherent in mobile gaming. While ostensibly harmless, its immersive nature and pervasive influence have the power to disrupt lives and perpetuate harmful behaviors. As society grapples with the implications of digital technology on everyday life, it is imperative to remain vigilant against the encroachment of virtual realities into the fabric of our existence. Only by fostering a balanced approach to technology consumption can we mitigate the adverse effects of apps like BitLife and reclaim control over our real-world destinies.

in:
BitLife, Activities, Features, Gameplay
Activities
Sign in to edit
History
Purge
Talk (0)
Activities
Launched on iOS:
September 2018 (default)
Launched on Android:
February 2019 (default)
Activities are one of the most important mechanics of BitLife, as from here the player will have access to further gameplay, which gives your character things to do and shape them for Ribbons. Activities are greyed out if the character is too young to do them, and will stop being greyed out when they reach the minimum age to do these activities. Many activities can have mixed results and some include scenarios with them. Some actions are dependent on the nation your character is from, such as the driving age per nation, and the laws on doing gambling. You can add activities into Favourite Activities in the Settings which put them at the very top, which can reduce time scrolling and searching for an activity you commonly do.
Activity
Description
Options
Minimum age to do activities
Accessories
Accessorize yourself
Putting on eyewear or headwear prompts the achievement "Fancypants".
Eyewear (Put on some eyewear): You can put on eyewear that can be obtained in Challenges or Scavenger Hunts.
Headwear (Put on some headwear): You can put on headwear that can be obtained in Challenges or Scavenger Hunts.
0
Adoption
Adopt a child
Pick from up to 6 juveniles to adopt. Each have their own price, stats, looks, and reason why their birth parent(s) could not keep them. Some may be from foreign Nations.
18 (available)
21 (official)

Commit a crime
Bank Robbery
Burglary
Delinquent
Embezzle*
Grand theft auto
Hitman
Murder
Pickpocket
Porch pirate
Shoplift
Train Robbery
* You must have a full-time job in order to embezzle.
8 (Delinquent, Pickpocket, Porch Pirate, Shoplift)
10 (Burglary)
14 (Grand Theft Auto and Murder)
16 (Bank Robbery, Embezzle, and Train Robbery)
18 (Hitman)
Doctor
Visit the doctor
Alternative Doctor (Seek alternative medicine)
Doctor (Seek western medicine): You can choose between two different doctors, each with varying levels of quality. If you do not have a disease/ailment, the doctor will not treat you. If you do, the doctor will treat you, and there is a chance your disease/ailment gets cured. It is free to have an appointment with a doctor or be treated by them in certain countries.
Donate Blood (Make a blood donation):
Donate Plasma (Make a plasma donation):
Emergency Room (Visit the emergency room): You can go there and be treated. Being treated in the emergency room would increase your health if it is low. It is free to be treated in the emergency room in certain countries.
Optometrist (Visit the optometrist): The optometrist can only be accessed with Bitizenship. You can select to put on different spectacles or contact lenses there.
Psychiatrist (Visit a shrink):
Witch Doctor (Seek the last resort): You can get the Witchcraft achievement if your disease is cured after being prescribed by the witch doctor.
4 (Doctor and Emergency Room)
18 (Alternative Doctor, Psychiatrist, Witch Doctor, Blood, Plasma Donation)
Emigrate
Move to another country
The player will have an option of eight nations to emigrate to.
18 (19 in countries where the high school graduation age is 19 unless dropped out)
Fame (Only available if famous)
Manage your fame
Book
Commercial
Photo Shoot
Talk Show
18 (Though you'd usually need to be a little older to gain enough fame)
Fertility
Visit a fertility clinic
Birth Control (Female)
IVF
Sperm Donor(Females only)
Tubal Ligation (Female)
Vasectomy (Male)
Sperm Donation(Males only)
14 (Birth Control)
18 (IVF, Sperm Donor, Tubal Ligation/Vasectomy,Sperm Donation)
Gamble
Visit the casino
Choose one of two casinos (casino names are randomized) and play minigames of Blackjack. In some countries, gambling is illegal.
18
Horse Races
Go to the horse track
Choose to bet from five horses or just watch.
18
Identity
Define your identity
Gender (Identify your gender): When clicked, a tab will open stating your character's desired gender and their birth gender. The player can choose to identify as cisgender (same as birth gender), genderqueer (gender that constantly changes/existing outside of male/female), non-binary (neither male or female), or transgender male/or female (not the same as birth gender). Identifying as transgender prompts your character to change your name to one suiting your gender.
Name Change
Sexuality
4 (Gender)
Lawsuit
Sue someone
Your character can sue surgeons that botch your plastic surgery, people who assault you, or police who falsely accuse you of crime.
18
Licences
Manage your licenses
Get a driver's, pilot's, or boating licence. The game will show a traffic sign, plane signal, or boating scenario and the player will have to choose the correct meaning of it to pass the exam. Before getting a pilot's licence, your character must attend flight school and get at least 40 hours.
Dependent on the legal ages in the country your character is from.
(15-19)
18 (all other licenses)
Lottery
Play the lottery
Your character can buy lottery tickets to win a jackpot prize. They may buy 1 or 10 tickets. Winning the lottery is rare, but doing so wins the "Jackpot" achievement.
18
Love
Find someone to love
Date
Dating app
Hookup
Threesome
Mail order bride(Males only)
14 (Date)
18 (Dating app, Hookup, Threesome,Mail order bride)
Mind & Body
Work on self-improvement
Acting Lessons
Book (Find a good book): The player is given a selection of seven different books varying in length. After choosing a book, a tab states the title, the author, the genre, and the amount of pages it has. Tapping the book flips one page. After tapping it as much as the number of pages in the book, you can finish the book, which increases your smarts and happiness. You can also choose to abandon the book or pick a different book, as well as watching the movie the book is associated with, which requires you to watch an ad if you don't have Bitizenship. Finishing the dictionary prompts the achievement "Human Dictionary".
Diet
Gardening
Gym
Instruments: You can pay to take lessons to learn how to play certain instruments, or ask your parents to let your character take instrument lessons, which they can decline if they have little money or if you already play a lot of different instruments. After taking a lesson, your skill on the instrument would improve/worsen (which happens if you take too many lessons in the same year), and your parents can buy you instruments if you are under 18. When the skill bar is full, you "master" it. These are all the different instruments you can choose to take lessons on:
Accordion;
Banjo;
Bass Guitar;
Cello;
Didgeridoo (mastering it prompts the "Didgeridoo'er" achievement);
Drums;
Flute;
Guitar;
Harmonica;
Harp;
Kazoo;
Keyboard;
Organ;
Pan Flute (mastering it prompts the "Pan Flute Pro" achievement);
Piano;
Recorder;
Saxophone;
Steel Drum;
Tambourine;
Triangle;
Trombone;
Trumpet;
Tuba;
Violin.
Library
Martial Arts
Meditate
Memory Test (Have your memory evaluated): The player can play a minigame where they must repeat the sequence of flashing lights and colors. The sequence gets longer for each correct repeated sequence, making the game harder. Scoring less than 10 correct sequences will decrease smarts, while scoring more than 10 will increase it. Scoring a 20 on the memory test prompts the achievement "Hyperthymesia".
Modeling Lessons
Space Camp
Walks
Voice Lessons
6 (Book, Instruments, Memory Test)
8 (Walk, Martial Arts,Acting&Voice Lessons)
12 (Garden, Gym, Library, Meditation)
18 (Diet)
Movie Theater
Go to the movies
Watching a movie prompts the achievement "Moviegoer". Watching five movies prompts the achievement "Movie Junkie". Watching over twenty movies throughout your life gets the player the "Movie Buff" ribbon.
Comedy (Go see a comedy):
Documentary (Go see a documentary):
Family (Go see a children's film): You can watch a film with your family, which increases happiness.

Go clubbing
Choose from 3 nightclubs.
18

Pet Store

Face Lift
Liposuction

Sex Change Surgery

Pray
Pray to the BitLife devs
To pray, you need to choose a subject to pray about and watch an ad. Alternatively, you can choose to 'curse them instead,' which usually ends up breaking the game. Currently, you can pray about six subjects: Fertility, General, Happiness, Health, Love, and Wealth. You need to be at least 18 years old to pray. When it was first introduced, you could pray for a clue about the next update. However, when BitLife announced the update, this option was removed.

Alcoholics Anonymous
Hypnotherapy
Rehab Center
18
Royalty (Only available if in the royal family)
Manage your royal life
Abdicate
Celebrity
Execute (Only available when a monarch)

To surrender, the player will end their character's life, ending their journal with "I surrendered" and giving the Wasteful ribbon. Before choosing to surrender, the game will warn "Don't end the life of (character name)!" and provide the option to continue their life or to surrender. Previously known as "End Life," the journal ended with "I ended my life," and the character would not receive the Wasteful ribbon unless they surrendered at age 0 or contributed little to their life.

Game Mode resolution when connected to TV/Monitor
I'm surprised it's still not possible to choose a resolution in game mode. Even soon to be two decade old consoles can do it.

正在显示第 16 - 30 条，共 50 条留言
